title: Keep generated Rails output handwritten-looking and isolate compiler metadata
description: RailsHx generated Ruby/Rails artifacts should look like idiomatic hand-written Rails code by default. Current output still exposes compiler internals such as __hx_rails_schema on ActiveRecord models and avoidable __hx* temporaries in app-facing code. Move required typed metadata to isolated RailsHx support surfaces/manifests or generated support modules, remove avoidable compiler temps, and require inline generated comments for any remaining scaffolding that is semantically necessary.
acceptance_criteria: Generated Rails models/controllers/views no longer expose avoidable __hx_* methods or compiler temp names; required schema/typed metadata lives in an isolated RailsHx namespace/manifest/support module; any unavoidable scaffolding has an inline generated comment explaining why it exists and what consumes it; snapshots and runtime tests enforce the cleaned output shape.
notes: Completed: generated Rails output now writes app-facing Rails artifacts to idiomatic Rails paths, removes runtime __hx_rails_schema / typed_column_count model pollution, keeps typedColumnCount as a compile-time literal helper, and avoids first-use __hx0 local suffixes in generated Ruby unless a real collision/internal compiler need remains. Added/updated smoke checks and snapshots to reject the old schema pollution and validate the cleaner generated shape. Verification: npm test passed green.

title: Implement typed ActionMailer parameterized mailer contracts
description: Add the first typed RailsHx parameterized-mailer slice. Mailer classes should declare a params typedef through metadata or a typed base/facade, mailer bodies should access params through typed helpers, and generated delivery wrappers should lower to ordinary Rails UserMailer.with(...).action delivery chains.
acceptance_criteria: A Haxe-authored mailer can declare required params and read them without Dynamic; generated Ruby uses ActionMailer params[:key] inside the mailer and UserMailer.with(key: value).action for delivery; Haxe compile tests reject missing/wrong parameter values and unknown params; unchecked dynamic params remain an explicit escape hatch; action-mailer smoke snapshots assert generated Ruby shape and docs show the supported API.
close_reason: Implemented @:railsMailerParams typed parameterized mailer contracts: the ActionMailer base build macro now generates withParams(...) for Rails .with(...) and generated p.<field> MailParam tokens from a params typedef, compiler lowering keeps params[:key] output clean, the action_mailer example uses the generated API, docs/inventory/compatibility were updated, and focused ActionMailer smoke passes.

title: Implement typed ActionMailer preview classes
description: Add RailsHx-authored ActionMailer preview support that emits normal Rails ActionMailer::Preview classes under test/mailers/previews. Preview methods should call typed mailer delivery wrappers so preview params/templates remain checked.
acceptance_criteria: @:railsMailerPreview or equivalent emits ActionMailer::Preview Ruby in the Rails preview path; preview methods can return typed mailer message deliveries; generated preview Ruby is snapshotted and ruby -c checked; REQUIRE_RAILS=1 action-mailer runtime lane loads or invokes at least one generated preview; unsupported Rails-owned preview adoption remains documented as an interop boundary.
close_reason: Implemented Haxe-authored ActionMailer previews: added rails.action_mailer.Preview, @:railsMailerPreview compiler-erased artifact emission under test/mailers/previews, safe preview path validation and duplicate detection, an action_mailer preview example using typed withParams, committed preview snapshots, ruby -c coverage, and Rails runtime loading/invocation in the action-mailer smoke.

title: Implement typed ActionCable connection auth and identifiers
description: Implement the typed RailsHx ActionCable connection/auth design from haxe.ruby-bee: @:railsCableConnection classes, identified_by declarations, typed connection identifiers, assign/access helpers, and reject_unauthorized_connection lowering.
acceptance_criteria: std exposes rails.action_cable.Connection and ConnectionIdentifier<T> with documented typed helpers; @:railsCableConnection emits normal ActionCable::Connection::Base Ruby with identified_by declarations from static final identifiers = { identifiedBy(...) }; connect() and helper methods compile normally; assign(identifier, value) lowers to self.<identifier> = value; Channel.connection(identifier) or equivalent exposes typed identifiers inside @:railsChannel classes without Dynamic; rejectUnauthorizedConnection() lowers to reject_unauthorized_connection; ActionCable snapshots include generated connection output; negative compile tests reject raw string identifiers and channels accessing undeclared identifiers; generated Rails ActionCable::Connection::TestCase runtime coverage asserts accepted and rejected connections; docs and compatibility/package checks are updated.
close_reason: Implemented typed ActionCable connection support: Connection, ConnectionIdentifier<T>, ConnectionParam<T>, ConnectionDecl, CableConnectionDsl, ConnectionMacro, @:railsCableConnection Ruby emission, identified_by lowering, assign/requestParam/channel connection access lowering, ActionCable sample coverage, negative raw identifier checks, committed snapshots, runtime ActionCable::Connection::TestCase accepted/rejected coverage, docs, inventory, and package checks.

title: Add RailsHx compile-then-delegate Rails tasks
description: Add RailsHx Rake tasks that refresh generated Haxe/HHX artifacts before delegating to Rails tasks, and document that watch mode keeps target artifacts current during development.
acceptance_criteria: Generated apps expose compile-then-delegate tasks for db migrate/prepare/rollback, tests, and arbitrary Rails task delegation; docs explain raw Rails tasks remain valid when artifacts are current; package/generator smoke tests assert the task surface.
close_reason: Implemented compile-then-delegate RailsHx Rake tasks for db migrate/prepare/rollback, generic Rails task delegation, and tests; documented workflow and updated package/generator smoke coverage.

title: Polish todoapp Devise/admin UI buttons and spacing
description: Fix unstyled native buttons and awkward vertical spacing in the RailsHx todoapp Devise/admin surfaces. Ensure login/logout/guest/admin CRUD controls use the app button system, panels have intentional spacing at desktop and mobile widths, and RailsHx HHX remains the source of truth.
close_reason: Polished todoapp Devise/admin UI: styled Rails button_to outputs whether classes land on form/input/button, added user-frame breathing room, tightened admin panel spacing, and verified with todoapp static smoke plus Playwright browser gate.

title: Keep chat status live after Turbo stream replacement
description: The todoapp chat panel currently uses a manual client catch-up path: Haxe JS fetches a Turbo Stream snapshot, calls Turbo.renderStreamMessage, then waits with Async.delay(0) before rebinding hooks/status. That timer is a smell and is not needed in a classic Hotwire app. Replace this with the Rails-native shape: typed HHX <turbo_stream_from stream=...>, server-side TurboStreams.broadcast*To(...) rendering a typed HHX partial, and minimal Haxe JS only for progressive form/status polish where Rails does not already provide the behavior.
notes: Fixed by replacing todoapp custom ChatMessagesChannel/client catch-up with typed HHX turbo_stream_from plus server-rendered Turbo Stream broadcasts. Restored visible connection status by styling Turbo Rails native turbo-cable-stream-source[connected], not by adding custom subscription JS. Verified with todoapp smoke, Rails tests, and Playwright.

title: Expand todoapp_rails into a richer RailsHx dogfood app
description: Grow examples/todoapp_rails from a typed todo slice into a richer, beautiful RailsHx app that demonstrates production-relevant Rails surfaces: users, session/login flow, roles/user management, chatroom/realtime-style interactions, richer Turbo/importmap JS patterns, and well-tested Rails runtime/browser behavior.
acceptance_criteria: Todoapp remains beautiful and fast; source of truth stays Haxe/HHX for RailsHx-owned artifacts; first-party auth/session and roles are implemented without hard-coding Devise until the DeviseHx/gem-layer bead exists; later Devise integration can be added as an explicit gem-layer example; app illustrates typed models, associations, migrations, controllers, routes, strong params, HHX templates, components, Turbo frames/streams/client JS, Rails tests, Haxe-authored tests, and Playwright; README documents start/watch/prod/test flows; each new feature has Rails-style unit/request/browser coverage and comments in example source explaining the type-safety feature being exercised.
notes: Completed richer todoapp dogfood expansion across two slices. First slice added typed users, session/login flow, roles/user management, Haxe-owned user/session routes, HHX user switcher, Rails tests, and Haxe client session hooks. Second slice added typed ChatMessage model, CreateChatMessages migration snapshot, ChatMessagesController, Haxe-owned chat_messages route, ChatPanel HHX partial, Turbo Stream replacement flow, Haxe client chat hooks, seed data, CSS polish, Rails model/request tests, Playwright chat posting, and snapshot/static smoke coverage. True multi-browser ActionCable live broadcast is intentionally split to haxe.ruby-bjv.15 as optional deeper dogfood.
close_reason: Todoapp now demonstrates typed users/session/roles/user management plus a typed Turbo-backed chatroom with model, migration, controller, routes, strong params, HHX, Haxe JS hooks, Rails tests, Playwright, and snapshots. Optional true ActionCable live chat follow-up filed as haxe.ruby-bjv.15.

title: Add generator-assisted RailsHx gem-layer adoption
description: Design and implement a RailsHx installed-gem adoption lane so apps can generate typed Haxe contracts for Bundler-installed Ruby/Rails gems, with Devise as the guiding production example. Runtime ownership stays with the Ruby gem; RailsHx owns typed contracts, macros, validation, and generated Haxe package structure.
acceptance_criteria: A future bin/rails generate hxruby:adopt --gem or hxruby:gem-layer command discovers installed gems through Bundler, fails closed for missing/unsafe gem paths, reads deterministic metadata such as RBS/YARD/source/routes without executing arbitrary app code, emits review-marked typed Haxe contracts instead of unchecked Dynamic, supports reusable companion package extraction for popular gems, includes a DeviseHx design/example covering model modules, controller helpers, routes, and current_user typing, and documents the workflow with tests for missing gem, generated contract compile, and Rails runtime/parity seams.
notes: Implemented the deterministic first slice of gem-layer adoption: hxruby:adopt accepts --gem NAME --discover and --gem NAME --write contracts; discovery resolves installed gems through Bundler without requiring gem source, parses Ruby source with Ripper instead of execution, fails closed for missing Gemfile/missing gems/unsafe names, emits review-marked app-local Haxe contracts and docs, updates generated app docs, and extends rails-adopt-generator smoke coverage for discovery, contract compile, manifest entries, and failure cases. Remaining reusable companion-package/DeviseHx work moved to haxe.ruby-bjv.6.4. Before implementing that companion-package/DeviseHx portion, ask GPT 5.5 Pro for a focused design review using this repo, ../haxe.elixir.codex, ../haxe.compilerdev.reference/rails, ../haxe.compilerdev.reference/ruby, and target gem source/docs as references.
close_reason: Completed deterministic installed-gem adoption slice: --gem discovery and --write contracts are implemented, fail-closed, documented, and covered by rails-adopt-generator smoke tests. Reusable DeviseHx companion package hardening is split to haxe.ruby-bjv.6.4 with GPT 5.5 Pro pre-research required.

title: Generate RailsHx starter app skeleton and start tasks
description: Make the RailsHx install/app generator create a useful typed Rails starter skeleton and default Rake start/watch tasks, then document the workflow from README and RailsHx docs.
acceptance_criteria: Generated RailsHx app includes typed controller, HHX layout/page, Haxe-owned routes, route extern placeholder, client JS, CSS, importmap, Rake tasks, and bin helpers; installed app exposes bundle exec rake hxruby:start and hxruby:start:watch; generator smoke/package/release contract tests cover the skeleton and tasks; README/docs/examples explain the generated files and dev/prod workflows.
close_reason: Implemented RailsHx starter app skeleton, app-level start/watch tasks, route alias, docs, and generator smoke compile coverage.

title: Add one-command todoapp start task
description: Add a one-command Rake todoapp start workflow that prepares and starts the generated Rails app, plus an integrated watch mode that runs the Rails server and RailsHx watcher together.
acceptance_criteria: rake todoapp:start prepares and starts the todoapp server; WATCH=1 rake todoapp:start and rake todoapp:start:watch prepare once and run server plus watcher with cleanup on exit; docs and todoapp script hints use the new Rake commands.
close_reason: Added rake todoapp:start as the one-command prepare-and-server path, plus WATCH=1 rake todoapp:start, rake todoapp:start:watch, and rake 'todoapp:start[watch]' for an integrated server+watcher loop with process cleanup. Updated README/todoapp docs and script hints.

title: Expose RailsHx repo/todoapp workflows through Rake
description: Add root Rake entrypoints for RailsHx repo-facing Rails/todoapp workflows so Ruby/Rails users can run rake todoapp:* and rake rails:* instead of npm scripts. Keep npm for CI/Node-only internals.
acceptance_criteria: Root Rakefile exposes todoapp compile/prepare/server/watch/test/production/playwright tasks, Rails generator wrapper tasks, format/security/package helpers, and docs present Rake as the RailsHx/todoapp happy path while preserving npm as CI/internal glue.
close_reason: Added root Rakefile with RailsHx/todoapp/generator/format/security/package/test wrappers; updated README/todoapp/docs to present Rake as the Rails/RailsHx-facing UX while preserving npm as Node/CI glue; release contracts now verify the Rake-facing docs and root Rakefile.

title: Switch greenfield generator route defaults after gates pass
description: Child of haxe.ruby-2pd. After route parity/tests/docs pass, update greenfield RailsHx generators to default to Haxe-owned routes while preserving Rails-owned route sync for existing apps and adoption.
acceptance_criteria: Greenfield RailsHx app/scaffold generators support --routes=haxe|snippet|rails|none, default to --routes=haxe only for newly generated RailsHx apps, and never silently mutate unowned config/routes.rb. Existing/adopted Rails apps default to --routes=snippet or --routes=rails. Scaffold route behavior depends on haxe.ruby-2pd.5 parity. Generated todoapp uses Haxe-owned routes and route externs generated from Rails output. Docs and help output explain the modes.
close_reason: Greenfield app and scaffold generators now support --routes=haxe|snippet|rails|none. App/install defaults to Haxe-owned routes while allowing snippet, Rails-owned, or no-route modes. Scaffold defaults to Haxe-owned AppRoutes for controller scaffolds, keeps Rails-owned helper extern behavior under --routes=rails, writes reviewable route instructions under --routes=snippet, and leaves route files untouched under --routes=none. Rails generator wrappers expose the same option, smoke tests cover all modes, docs/help explain the source-of-truth choices, and todoapp remains Haxe-owned with route externs generated from Rails output.

title: Update routing docs and examples
description: Child of haxe.ruby-2pd. Document Haxe-owned routing, Rails-owned routing, source-of-truth inversion, generated-artifact ownership, parity checks, and examples once the route DSL implementation lands.
acceptance_criteria: docs/railshx-routing-design.md, docs/railshx-roadmap.md, docs/railshx-generators-and-tasks-design.md, docs/railshx-generated-artifact-ownership.md, docs/railshx-controller-guide.md, docs/railshx-testing-strategy.md, README links, and examples/todoapp_rails route docs are updated. Examples explain which type-safety features are exercised, what IntelliSense exposes, and which Rails artifacts are emitted. Todoapp demonstrates Haxe-owned routes end-to-end while Rails-owned route sync remains documented for adoption.
notes: Todoapp now demonstrates mixed route ownership: AppRoutes.hx owns the greenfield routes, a documented Rails-owned routes_rails_owned.rb snippet models an existing Rails route, generated Routes.hx exposes legacyHealthPath(), and typed HHX consumes that helper from Haxe. Full routing docs/examples for every route mode remain open under this bead.
close_reason: Updated routing docs and examples for the implemented Haxe-owned/Rails-owned route contract. Added examples/rails_routes_dsl README, linked the focused route snapshot fixture from README/docs, aligned railshx-routing-design with generated headers/manifests and implemented evidence, documented route generator modes, and cross-linked routing from controller, roadmap, ownership, testing, and todoapp docs.

title: Add route snapshots and negative compile tests
description: Child of haxe.ruby-2pd. Add compiler snapshots, generated Routes.hx snapshots, negative compile tests, Rails request tests, Rails-owned fixture tests, and generated-artifact ownership tests for the routing DSL.
acceptance_criteria: Snapshots cover generated config/routes.rb and generated Routes.hx for root, verbs, typed targets, resources/resource, member/collection, namespace/scope/controller, optional/glob, constraints/defaults, mountExternal, and external controller refs. Negative compile tests cover missing/non-static routes, missing controller/action, invalid only/except action, newAction mapping errors, invalid aliases/paths/regexes/constants, member/collection outside resources, unchecked raw routes without define, duplicate output targets, and unowned config/routes.rb overwrite attempts. Rails request/runtime tests cover root dispatch, resource route, member/collection route, namespace, optional segment, and glob route.
notes:
2026-06-18 runtime route slice: expanded todoapp_rails Haxe-owned AppRoutes with resource collection/member routes, namespace helper output, optional segment route, and glob route. Added vanilla Rails RoutesTest coverage for root/resources/member/collection/optional/glob recognition, generated route helpers, and the Rails-owned legacy_health adoption route. Updated route extern fixture/snapshots and todoapp smoke checks. Real Rails todoapp test lane passes locally: 15 runs, 91 assertions, 0 failures.

2026-06-18 negative route DSL slice: added compile-failure coverage for missing routes field, non-static routes field, non-block routes initializer, invalid resources only/except actions, only+except conflicts, top-level member blocks, and Rails :new action mapping. Added compiler diagnostic that Haxe newAction route refs must be @:native("new") so generated Rails routes lower to :new; positive route DSL smoke now covers resources(... only: [newAction]) output.
close_reason: Completed route snapshot and negative compile test contract. Added a dedicated rails_routes_dsl snapshot fixture covering config/routes.rb and .railshx/routes.haxe.json for root, verbs, typed targets, resources/resource, member/collection, namespace/scope/controller, optional/glob paths, defaults/constraints, mountExternal, external controller refs, and newAction->:new mapping. Negative route DSL smoke covers missing/non-static/invalid routes declarations, missing controller/action, invalid only/except, newAction mapping, aliases/paths/regexes/constants, member/collection nesting, unchecked raw routes, duplicate outputs, and unowned overwrite attempts. Rails request/runtime coverage is present in todoapp_rails and passed locally.

title: Implement resources and scope edge coverage
description: Child of haxe.ruby-2pd. Fill out the first-slice Rails routing surface beyond basic root/verbs so the DSL is useful for real Rails apps without pretending to support arbitrary Ruby routes.
acceptance_criteria: Haxe-owned routing supports resource, resources, only, except, member, collection, namespace, scope path/module/as/defaults/constraints, controller blocks, optional segments, glob segments, param, simple defaults, simple constraints, checked mountExternal, and checked external controllers. Deferred surfaces such as concerns, shallow routes, drawExternal, redirect/direct/resolve, gem macros, arbitrary Ruby loops, and dynamic route generation are explicitly unsupported with diagnostics or documented follow-up beads.
close_reason: Route edge coverage is complete for this slice: Haxe-owned routing already supports resources/resource, only/except/param, member/collection, namespace/scope/controller, optional/glob paths, defaults/constraints, mountExternal, checked extern targets, and route manifests/parity. Added explicit RailsHx diagnostics and smoke coverage for deferred concerns, shallow routes, external route files, redirect/direct/resolve-style helpers, and gem macros so unsupported Rails surfaces fail clearly instead of as generic Haxe unknown identifiers.

title: Add Haxe-owned route sync and parity task
description: Child of haxe.ruby-2pd. Extend route helper sync so Haxe-owned mode compiles Haxe, asks Rails for the authoritative route table, regenerates Routes.hx, and compares Rails output with the Haxe route manifest.
acceptance_criteria: hxruby:routes supports rails-owned, haxe-owned, and auto modes. Haxe-owned mode runs Haxe compile first, runs bin/rails routes --expanded, regenerates Routes.hx using the existing generator path, and runs RoutesParity checks. Parity catches missing route, wrong target, wrong path, wrong verb, wrong explicit alias, and unsafe opaque raw routes. Rails-owned route helper sync remains backward compatible.
close_reason: Implemented hxruby:routes MODE=rails-owned|haxe-owned|auto. Haxe-owned mode compiles Haxe first, runs Rails routes, regenerates Routes.hx through the existing generator, and validates Rails output against .railshx/routes.haxe.json with the new RoutesParity checker. Parity smoke covers success plus missing route, wrong target, wrong path, wrong verb, and opaque raw-route rejection; Rails-owned route generator remains backward compatible.

title: Add compiler-owned route manifest and ownership recording
description: Child of haxe.ruby-2pd. Record Haxe-owned route output in a manifest and enforce generated-artifact ownership for config/routes.rb and route JSON artifacts.
acceptance_criteria: Compiler emits .railshx/routes.haxe.json or .railshx/routes/main.json with source, output, class, declarations, positions, and opaque unsafe entries. config/routes.rb writes are allowed only when absent, RailsHx-header-owned, or manifest-owned. Existing unowned config/routes.rb fails closed. Multiple @:railsRoutes classes targeting one output fail. Manifest/header ownership tests cover absent, generated rewrite, manifest rewrite, unsafe path, and unowned collision.
close_reason: Implemented Haxe-owned route manifest and ownership recording: compiler emits .railshx/routes.haxe.json with source/class/output/declarations/positions/opaque raw entries, records config/routes.rb and the route manifest in .railshx/manifest.json with checksums, allows manifest/header-owned rewrites, rejects unowned config/routes.rb collisions, and rejects duplicate @:railsRoutes outputs. routes-dsl smoke covers these cases.

title: Extract route IR and emit config/routes.rb
description: Child of haxe.ruby-2pd. Add compiler extraction for @:railsRoutes classes and emit readable Rails-native config/routes.rb from the route IR while suppressing Ruby generation for the declaration host.
acceptance_criteria: RubyCompiler detects @:railsRoutes only in Rails mode, extracts exactly one static final routes declaration, lowers it through RailsRoutesExtractor/RailsRoutesEmitter/RailsRouteManifest, emits config/routes.rb with RailsHx header/source comments, preserves route declaration order, emits ordinary Rails routing DSL, and produces no AppRoutes Ruby class. title: Design current-schema validation for historical migration snapshots
description: Historical migration snapshots and current ActiveRecord model metadata can intentionally differ. For example, a current model may include a field that an older AddColumn snapshot introduced, while the migration compiler currently treats knownModels as the validation source for both current schema and new-column checks. Design and implement a validation mode that preserves compile-time diagnostics without falsely rejecting legitimate migration history.
acceptance_criteria: RailsHx distinguishes current model/schema validation from historical migration operation validation; AddColumn snapshots generated before/alongside current model fields can compile without disabling table/column safety entirely; docs explain when to use knownModels, externalTables, schema snapshots, or future migration-history metadata; todoapp or a focused smoke covers a current model field introduced by an older snapshot; diagnostics remain fail-closed for genuinely unknown tables/columns.
close_reason: Implemented current-schema vs historical migration snapshot validation: knownModels still validates known tables/read-column references, but AddColumn newness is checked against same-snapshot columns instead of today’s model schema, allowing historical AddColumn snapshots for current model fields while duplicate same-migration additions still fail. Added focused todoapp smoke coverage and docs.

title: Harden SQL/string policy CI gate for RailsHx canonical sources
description: Strengthen npm run test:sql-string-policy so it checks canonical RailsHx source and docs for accidental raw query strings, verifies every allowed explicit SQL/string escape is documented in the escape-hatch audit, and keeps future Sql.* work auditable. The gate should keep raw ActiveRecord query fragments rejected unless a line carries a deliberate railshx:allow-raw-sql-example marker inside policy docs/examples.
close_reason: Hardened npm run test:sql-string-policy to scan canonical Haxe examples plus selected Markdown code fences for raw ActiveRecord query strings, requiring deliberate railshx:allow-raw-sql-example markers for documented negative examples. The gate now also asserts explicit escape hatches @:railsAllowRawErb, Template.external, Lock.custom, and externalTables are documented in the escape-hatch audit.

title: Add explicit RailsHx Sql escape hatch APIs
description: Define explicit, auditable SQL escape hatches for cases the typed Expr/Predicate builders cannot yet represent. Candidate APIs include Sql.raw/fragment/literal with scary names, strict docs, examples, and negative tests proving casual strings remain rejected on where/order/select/group/having. Prefer typed builders first; raw SQL must be opt-in and easy for audits/LLMs to find.
notes: Implementing explicit RailsHx SQL escape hatch APIs: Sql<TModel,TKind>, SqlWhere/SqlOrder phantom kinds, whereSql/whereNotSql/orderSql/reorderSql relation entrypoints, compiler lowering, smoke tests, docs, and SQL policy coverage.
close_reason: Implemented explicit RailsHx SQL escape hatch APIs: Sql<TModel,TKind> with SqlWhere/SqlOrder phantom kinds, whereSql/whereNotSql/orderSql/reorderSql, compiler lowering to Rails query methods, negative smoke tests for plain strings/wrong owner/wrong kind, docs, inventory, SQL policy gate updates, and refreshed snapshots.

title: Implement RailsHx Expr and Predicate core
description: Add rails.active_record.Expr<TModel,TValue> and Predicate<TModel> as typed Haxe-only tokens. Support Expr.field(...), Expr.lower(...), comparison predicate methods, Relation.whereExpr(...), Relation.whereNotExpr(...), and compiler lowering to Rails/Arel-backed Ruby. Keep casual strings fail-closed: no from String conversions, owner/value-type mismatches fail at Haxe compile time, and examples include negative compile tests.
notes: Implementing first concrete RailsHx typed expression slice from the SQL/string policy: Expr<TModel,TValue>, Predicate<TModel>, Relation.whereExpr/whereNotExpr, and typed lower(...) ordering. This bead is intentionally narrow; richer functions, aggregate aliases, having, and explicit Sql.* escapes are tracked separately.
close_reason: Implemented RailsHx Expr<TModel,TValue> and Predicate<TModel> tokens, model/relation whereExpr/whereNotExpr stubs, compiler lowering to Rails/Arel predicates, positive ActiveRecord examples, negative compile coverage for raw strings/wrong owners/wrong value types/non-predicate shapes, docs, inventory, and snapshots.

title: Design RailsHx typed query expression layer backed by Arel
description: Design a stable RailsHx Expr/Predicate query layer for SQL function ordering and predicates. Public Haxe API should expose typed Expr<TModel,TValue>, Predicate<TModel>, and narrow builders such as Expr.lower(Todo.f.title), not raw Arel. Document tradeoffs for model ownership, value typing, relation chaining, generated Ruby shape, and interaction with the SQL/string escape policy. Include examples for order, whereExpr, whereNotExpr, future select/group/having, and explicit Sql.* escapes.
notes: Design remains open for the broader expression layer. First implemented slice establishes the public direction: RailsHx Expr/Predicate are typed Haxe APIs, Arel is generated Ruby backend detail, casual strings stay rejected, and future work should add typed builders/aggregates/having before explicit Sql.* escapes. Use the active_record_model smoke and docs/railshx-query-guide.md as the current contract.
close_reason: Completed RailsHx typed query expression design: documented Expr<TModel,TValue>/Predicate<TModel> as the public API, Arel as generated Ruby backend detail, model/value type safety, SQL escape policy through Sql.unsafe*, generated Ruby shape, and constraints for projection/grouping/having follow-up work. Linked from query guide, SQL/string policy, projection/grouping design, and roadmap; sql-string-policy CI now guards the design doc.

title: Implement typed SQL function ordering via RailsHx Expr.lower
description: Implement typed function ordering such as Todo.order(Expr.lower(Todo.f.title).asc()) and Order.lower(Todo.f.title).asc(). Lower generated Ruby to model.arel_table[:column].lower.asc so app code avoids raw order strings like "LOWER(title) ASC". Add smoke tests and docs showing that lower only accepts String fields from the same model owner.
close_reason: Implemented typed SQL function ordering with Expr.lower(Todo.f.title).asc() and Order.lower(...).asc(), lowering to Models::Todo.arel_table[:title].lower.asc. Added smoke assertions, negative compile tests for raw order strings/wrong model/non-string fields, docs, inventory, and snapshots.

title: Integrate RailsHx ActiveRecord SQL/string escape policy
description: Follow-up from haxe.ruby-bjv.3.1. Apply the approved SQL/string-bearing API policy to ActiveRecord query APIs that cannot be fully represented by field refs, such as raw where/order/select/group/having fragments and Arel-like expressions.
acceptance_criteria: ActiveRecord raw SQL/string APIs are either replaced by typed builders or explicit audited escape hatches; strict-boundary tests reject casual strings/raw SQL in production examples; docs explain safe usage and generated Ruby shape.
notes: RailsHx SQL/string policy hardening now includes typed Aggregate expression builders, Group.countHaving aggregate having predicates, and Projection.group selected grouped aggregate result rows. Raw having/projection strings remain rejected in examples/tests; remaining query SQL policy work is richer grouped keys, joined aggregate projections, broader SQL function builders, and any explicit Sql.* escapes for gaps.
close_reason: Completed ActiveRecord SQL/string policy hardening: raw SQL remains behind explicit Sql.unsafe* escapes; typed criteria/field refs cover normal where/order/projection/group/having paths; added fluent FieldTools helpers such as Todo.f.title.lower().eq("ship") and Todo.f.id.count().gt(1), compiler lowering to Rails/Arel, positive/negative smoke coverage, docs, inventory/package coverage, and snapshots.

title: Add RailsHx nested association criteria and nested joins/includes

acceptance_criteria: Typed nested association criteria and nested association loading APIs are designed, implemented, documented, and covered by active_record_model compile/runtime-shape tests; wrong association owners and invalid nested fields fail during Haxe compilation.
notes: Implemented typed nested association loading slice: Association.nested(parent, child) with Haxe owner/target chain validation; model/relation preload and eagerLoad APIs; compiler lowering to Rails includes({user: :todos}), preload({user: :todos}), and eager_load({user: :todos}); active_record_model positive/negative smoke coverage and docs. Remaining under this bead: nested association criteria design/implementation for where/findBy/exists-style filters.
close_reason: Implemented typed nested association criteria for where/findBy/exists with nested Rails hash lowering, active_record_model docs/examples, snapshot coverage, and invalid nested field/type compile-failure smoke tests.

close_reason: Implemented typed Rails scope declarations with @:railsScope and @:railsDefaultScope, Rails scope/default_scope artifact emission, active_record_model positive and negative coverage, snapshots, and docs.

title: Harden RailsHx generator and LLM-assisted inference security
description: Lock down generator-assisted Rails adoption so inferred contracts are deterministic, reviewable, and fail-closed.
acceptance_criteria: RBS/YARD/Ripper/Rails metadata inputs fail closed when missing or unsafe; discovery modes remain suggest-only unless explicitly writing checked wrappers; LLM assistance is documented as advisory patches only; generated Dynamic placeholders are marked for review; tests cover missing files, overwrite protection, and no execution of app code during inference.
close_reason: Completed generator/LLM-assisted inference hardening: adoption inputs that emit Haxe source now validate package names, Ruby constants, template paths, local names, and Haxe type strings; source/RBS/extension files must stay inside the app/output root and fail closed when missing; source-backed inference remains Ripper/RBS parsing only and does not execute app code; existing wrappers remain overwrite-protected; smoke coverage asserts unsafe inputs, missing files, overwrite protection, Dynamic review comments, and parser-only behavior. LLM assistance remains documented as advisory only.

title: Harden RailsHx template/path escape hatch enforcement
description: Make all RailsHx template/path escape hatches auditable and fail-closed by default.
acceptance_criteria: Template.of/layout/existing remain the default checked paths; Template.named/external and @:railsTemplate string paths are literal-only, safe-shape checked, and documented as lower-level escapes; missing external files fail closed where discovery is possible; unchecked escapes require comments/docs and negative tests; canonical samples avoid unchecked paths.
notes: Template/path hardening progressed: Component.of/existing validate slot names at macro time, Component.existing and Template.existing have missing-file negative coverage, and renderTemplateWithLayout now requires a typed Layout token. Template.layout(ViewClass) remains the default class-backed layout path; lower-level literals must use checked Layout.named(...); raw layout strings are rejected in todoapp smoke. Remaining: broader unchecked Template.named/external policy docs/tests and any additional generated-template path surfaces.
close_reason: Completed template/path escape hardening: Template.of/layout/existing remain the checked defaults; Template.named/external and @:railsTemplate paths are literal-only and safe-shape checked; Template.existing/Component.existing fail closed against app/views or rails/app/views; raw layout strings and unsafe paths have negative coverage; unchecked Template.external remains documented as a lower-level escape. This slice also rejects raw backslash paths across Template, Component, ViewMacro, MailerMacro, and HHX compiler lowering, with todoapp smoke regression coverage.

title: Harden RailsHx Dynamic boundary policy and replacements
description: Audit app-facing RailsHx Dynamic usage and replace ordinary production paths with typed facades, abstracts, or generated contracts.
acceptance_criteria: Dynamic usage is classified as runtime-required, compiler/internal, interop escape, or replacement target; canonical samples avoid app-level Dynamic except documented JS/Rails runtime boundaries; route params, Turbo details, ActionCable subscriptions, ActiveSupport subscriptions, and generator-produced externs have typed replacements or child beads.
notes: Implemented typed Rails route helper params with rails.routing.RouteParam. Added relation-level ActiveRecord find Dynamic hardening: Relation.find now uses Int/String overloads instead of Dynamic, rejects object-shaped IDs in active_record_model negative compile coverage, and emits direct Rails find(...) calls without helper runtime. Added Turbo client boundary hardening: TurboTypedEvent/TurboSubmitEvent expose EventTarget targets, Turbo.addFetchRequestHeader(...) wraps runtime-owned fetchOptions mutation, todoapp client no longer uses app-level Dynamic for submit targets/header mutation/storage catches, and turbo/todoapp smokes cover the helper. Added ActionCable client consumer hardening: Consumer.create() returns a nominal Consumer handle, Consumer.subscribe/TodosCableClient.subscribe require Consumer instead of Dynamic, raw object consumers fail in action-cable smoke, and docs/audit/inventory record the boundary. Added ActionCable perform hardening: Subscription is now an opaque JS handle facade, perform(...) requires typed Action<TData> tokens and matching payloads, performUnchecked(...) is the explicit legacy interop escape, and action-cable smoke rejects raw action strings/wrong payloads. Added ActionMailer options/attachments hardening: MailAddress and MailLayout replace Dynamic recipient/layout fields, MailRenderOptions types render kwargs, Attachments.add accepts typed string content, unchecked mailer escapes are explicit, action-mailer smoke rejects raw recipient objects/non-string attachments, and compiler keyword cleanup keeps abstract-backed kwargs Rails-shaped. Added ActiveSupport subscription handle hardening: Subscription is now opaque without implicit from Dynamic, unsubscribe({}) fails in instrumentation smoke, and docs/audit/inventory document the handle boundary. Added ActiveStorage attachable hardening: One.attach accepts String, Many.attach accepts Array<String>, raw Rails attachable payloads require explicit attachUnchecked(...), invalid object-shaped default attach fails in active-storage smoke, and docs/audit/inventory document the escape. Added controller Dynamic boundary hardening: direct ActionController render/redirect helpers now use RenderOptions/RedirectOptions with Status-typed status kwargs; redirectTo(String) remains the Rails path/location helper; redirectToOptions(...) handles Rails option-hash redirects; raw string statuses fail in action-controller negative compile coverage. Added request format Dynamic hardening: request().format() now returns typed RequestFormat instead of Dynamic, common MIME predicates are typed, and invalid assignment to String fails in action-controller negative compile coverage. Remaining Dynamic work includes generator-inferred review placeholders, broad Rails-owned render payloads such as json/locals/layout, and deeper controller lifecycle surfaces tracked by production gap beads.
close_reason: Completed Dynamic boundary hardening pass: app-facing Dynamic uses are classified in the escape-hatch audit, canonical RailsHx APIs use typed facades/abstracts/generated contracts, and remaining runtime-owned/generator/controller surfaces are documented under production follow-up beads. This slice also removes ActiveJob helper Dynamic returns: performNow preserves perform return type, performLater returns rails.active_job.Base, and active-job smoke now proves valid and invalid return typing.

title: Harden RailsHx SQL/string escape hatch enforcement

acceptance_criteria: ActiveRecord/migration/template/Turbo APIs classify strings as typed default, checked literal, or explicit escape; raw SQL fragments use named auditable APIs; strict-boundary tests reject accidental SQL/string drift in canonical samples; docs link each allowed escape to a safer typed alternative or follow-up bead.
notes: Initial enforcement slice complete via haxe.ruby-k25. Explicit SQL escape hatch API slice complete via haxe.ruby-elx. Turbo string-hardening complete for StreamTarget/StreamName. ActionCable string-hardening complete for Stream<TPayload>/SubscriptionParam<TValue>. Template/path hardening includes checked existing-template/component files, macro-validated component slot names, and typed Layout tokens rejecting raw layout strings. Controller lifecycle rescue constants now default to typed exception refs via rescueFrom(Type, handler); rescueFromNamed("Ruby::Constant", handler) is documented as a safe Ruby constant path escape and still validates the handler. Remaining work: broader migration string classifications and follow-up SQL surfaces such as select/group/having once those APIs are expanded.
close_reason: Completed SQL/string escape-hatch enforcement pass: ActiveRecord raw SQL routes through typed Sql.unsafeWhere/unsafeOrder relation APIs, canonical examples reject casual raw query strings, template/path/Turbo/ActionCable/controller string surfaces are classified and guarded, rescueFromNamed remains checked by safe Ruby constant paths, and migrations now validate externalTables as safe Rails table identifiers while preserving typed knownModels/operation checks. Remaining richer select/group/having/aggregate design work stays tracked by haxe.ruby-bjv.10, haxe.ruby-deg, and haxe.ruby-6ri.

acceptance_criteria: A documented policy classifies each string/SQL-bearing surface as typed default, checked literal, or explicit escape hatch; escape hatches are named and auditable; implementation beads for affected APIs reference the policy; strict-boundary tests reject accidental raw SQL/string drift in canonical examples.
close_reason: Documented RailsHx SQL/string-bearing policy with typed default, checked literal, and explicit escape-hatch classes; linked affected implementation beads; added npm run test:sql-string-policy to reject raw query strings in canonical examples.

close_reason: Closed realtime production-gap umbrella by adding generated Rails ActionCable::Channel::TestCase runtime coverage for subscribe/stream/perform/broadcast, keeping typed Turbo/ActionCable compile/browser gates intact, wiring ActionCable into npm run test:rails-runtime, and filing explicit follow-up beads for deeper ActionCable lifecycle/auth and broader Turbo frame/stream browser coverage.

close_reason: Closed production-gap umbrella by adding a generated Rails ActiveJob runtime lane with ActiveJob::TestHelper queue/enqueue/perform assertions under REQUIRE_RAILS=1, wiring it into npm run test:rails-runtime, documenting the runtime strategy, and filing explicit follow-up beads for remaining ActiveJob adapter/serialization/retry-discard work (haxe.ruby-71o), ActionMailer previews/parameterized/delivery tests (haxe.ruby-if3), and ActiveStorage runtime/direct-upload/storage helpers (haxe.ruby-cfj).

title: Close RailsHx controller, routing, and request lifecycle production gaps
description: Harden typed ActionController and routing coverage for real apps: before/after/around filters, redirects, respond_to, request variants, rescue handling, auth-friendly hooks, route/controller validation, and request specs.
acceptance_criteria: Controller APIs remain Rails-shaped and typed; route helpers and render/redirect/head/respond_to surfaces reject wrong arguments; request lifecycle hooks are method-backed where possible; errors/rescue and auth-oriented extension points are documented; Rails runtime request tests cover representative flows.
notes: Closed common controller request/response lifecycle blocker: added typed ordered MIME negotiation via rails.action_controller.Mime and request.negotiateMime(...), added typed request.setVariant([RequestVariantToken.*]) assignment, extended action-controller smoke with generated Ruby assertions and negative compile fixtures, refreshed std inventory/gap report/docs. Remaining auth/streaming/custom-CSRF breadth is non-blocking follow-up work.
close_reason: Closed

title: Close RailsHx model, validation, association, and migration production gaps
description: Harden model metadata and migration APIs for production Rails apps: validations, callbacks, associations, enums, schema evolution, indexes, constraints, reversible operations, and schema-history behavior.
acceptance_criteria: Common Rails validations/callbacks/association options are typed and documented; migration operations cover production schema changes including constraints/indexes/foreign keys/reversible paths; destructive or raw migration escapes are explicit; generated migrations pass Rails runtime and production dogfood checks.
notes:
Added a small Haxe-authored Rails request-test ergonomics slice. rails.test.Request now has compiler-erased responseBody() and responseMediaType() helpers lowering to ordinary Rails response.body/response.media_type. Todoapp Haxe request tests now assert rendered body content, completed route output, and file-route media/body responses from Haxe, with smoke expectations covering the generated Minitest. Updated stdlib inventory and Haxe-authored testing docs. Added root AGENTS hard rule that GitHub Actions on the pushed branch must be green before a slice is complete. Local gates passed: npm run test:todoapp-rails; npm run test:snapshots; npm run test:stdlib-inventory; npm run test:gap-report; npm run test:sql-string-policy; git diff --check. Next after push: verify GitHub Actions is green.
Follow-up after GitHub CI exposed runtime gaps on 12eaa3c: Rails validation regexes now lower ^/$ anchors to Rails-safe \A/\z inside @:validates metadata; scope-only HHX <field_errors> now guards form.object before reading errors; todoapp runtime migration no longer uses adapter-specific ValidateForeignKey/ValidateCheckConstraint steps in the SQLite-backed canonical app; Playwright user-role tests now use selectOption for generated <select> controls. Local gates passed: npm run format:haxe:check; npm run test:active-record-model; npm run test:todoapp-rails; npm run test:todoapp-production; npm run test:todoapp-playwright; npm run test:snapshots; npm run test:stdlib-inventory; npm run test:gap-report; npm run test:sql-string-policy; npm run test:rails-runtime. Next after push: verify latest GitHub Actions CI/Gitleaks are green.

title: Close RailsHx ActiveRecord query/scope/transaction production gaps
description: Implement the production ActiveRecord query surface beyond the current relation slice: typed scopes, transactions, locking, association-aware criteria, nested joins/includes, raw SQL policy, and safe escape hatches.
acceptance_criteria: Typed scope builders compose like Rails scopes; transactions and locking have Rails-shaped typed APIs; association-aware criteria and nested joins/includes are covered; string/SQL-bearing APIs follow the approved typed-or-explicit escape-hatch policy; active_record_model and Rails runtime/dogfood tests cover representative production queries.
notes: Implemented typed transaction and pessimistic locking slice: Todo.transaction(block, options), TransactionOptions/TransactionIsolation, relation/model lock(?Lock), compiler lowering to Rails transaction/lock calls, active_record_model docs/smoke/snapshots. Added relation-level find hardening: Relation.find now uses Int/String overloads instead of Dynamic while static model find remains primary-key typed. Remaining production gaps are tracked by sibling epic beads haxe.ruby-bjv.8 (nested association criteria/includes), haxe.ruby-bjv.9 (scope DSL), and haxe.ruby-bjv.10 (SQL/string policy integration).
close_reason: Closed the RailsHx ActiveRecord production query spine: typed scopes/default scopes, transactions, pessimistic locks, relation chains, nested association criteria/loading, fluent typed field predicates/aggregates, explicit SQL/string escape policy, active_record_model smoke coverage, docs, and snapshots are in place. Future less-common APIs such as unscope/only/except/references/strict_loading, multi-model projections, and richer grouped keys should be tracked as non-blocking follow-up breadth.

title: Make Rails runtime CI mandatory and broad
description: Harden Rails runtime confidence so production readiness does not rely on optional local skips.
acceptance_criteria: CI runs npm run test:rails-runtime as a required gate on supported Ruby/Rails lanes; missing Rails gems are hard failures in CI; runtime failures identify compiler/materialization/migration/request/browser stage; docs explain local vs CI behavior.
close_reason: Rails runtime CI is mandatory across the supported Ruby matrix, release contracts guard the lane, and runtime/browser smoke logs identify failing stages.

title: Add deployable RailsHx dogfood production app
description: Create or promote a RailsHx app that exercises production build/deploy concerns, not only smoke fixtures.
acceptance_criteria: A deployable app compiles Haxe/HHX/client JS, runs migrations, passes rails test, passes zeitwerk:check and assets:precompile, documents production build steps, and has a CI or scripted smoke proving the release artifact contains generated files.
close_reason: Added a CI-backed RailsHx production dogfood lane that compiles server/client Haxe, runs Rails migrations/tests, passes Zeitwerk/assets precompile, and validates a release archive with generated artifacts.

title: Audit and close RailsHx typed API production gaps
description: Inventory Rails/RailsHx API gaps that block realistic production apps and convert them into typed implementation beads.
acceptance_criteria: Gap audit covers ActiveRecord advanced queries/scopes/transactions/validations/migrations/controllers/templates/jobs/mailers/Turbo/ActionCable/ActiveStorage; each P0/P1 production blocker has a bead; raw string/SQL-bearing APIs have explicit typed or escape-hatch policy before implementation.
close_reason: Documented RailsHx typed API production gap audit, linked it from readiness/compatibility docs, and created P1 implementation beads for each production-blocking API surface plus SQL/string policy.

title: Audit production escape hatches and security policy
description: Make unsafe boundaries explicit before production use: raw Ruby, raw ERB, raw SQL/string clauses, Dynamic, unchecked template paths, external Ruby contracts.
acceptance_criteria: Strict-mode docs and tests cover raw __ruby__, raw ERB, SQL-ish strings, template/path filesystem checks, Dynamic boundaries, and generator inference; canonical samples avoid unsafe escapes; every allowed escape hatch is named, documented, opt-in, and has follow-up beads for typed replacements where appropriate.
close_reason: Documented RailsHx escape-hatch/security audit, linked it from production docs and AGENTS, verified existing strict/path/raw-ERB/adoption gates, and created P1 follow-up beads for SQL/string, Dynamic, path/template, and generator/LLM hardening.

title: Harden RailsHx generator and dev/prod UX
description: Ensure creating, adopting, developing, watching, testing, and producing RailsHx apps feels better than vanilla Rails plus manual Haxe ceremony.
acceptance_criteria: Install/scaffold/adopt/route generators are Ruby-native and tested; generated apps include clear bin/rake/dev/prod workflows; watch/client watch integrate with Rails conventions; errors are actionable; production compile/precompile flow is documented and smoke-tested.
close_reason: Added RailsHx production rake/bin workflow, generator/package/release smoke coverage, and updated dev/prod docs.

title: Implement typed Ruby mixin extension interop
description: Add first-class typed Ruby include/extend/prepend consumption for haxe.ruby. Support extension contract metadata that injects typed instance/static methods into Haxe classes and externs, emit include/extend/prepend for Haxe-owned classes, and cover positive/negative smoke tests. Follow-up monkey-patch using support and generator-assisted adoption separately.
close_reason: Implemented typed Ruby mixin extension contracts with include/prepend/extend emission, examples, docs, snapshots, and smoke coverage.

title: Validate RailsHx migration table and column references
description: Add compile-time validation for typed RailsHx migration operation table/column/index references where metadata is known. Cover unknown table/column diagnostics, DropTable positive reversible coverage, index reference validation, and docs for when external Rails-owned tables remain unchecked.
close_reason: Implemented typed RailsHx migration table/column/index/FK validation with knownModels/externalTables coverage and docs.

title: Validate RailsHx migration ordering and runtime execution
description: Add deterministic migration ordering checks, broader rollback/reversibility validation, and a Rails db:migrate runtime gate for generated RailsHx migrations when app gems are available. Cover invalid timestamp/order cases, foreign-key ordering, irreversible operation policy, and clear skip/require behavior for local environments without Rails gems.
notes: Implemented RailsHx migration registry validation: duplicate timestamps now fail, known foreign-key source/target tables created by later migrations fail, belongs_to-generated foreign keys participate in ordering checks, and rollback-hostile top-level ChangeColumn/RemoveColumn/DropTable operations must be wrapped in explicit Reversible(up, down). Rails integration now syntax-checks the generated update migration and continues to run db:migrate/test when app gems are available or required. Todoapp sample now models reversible change_column/foreign-key rollback and smoke tests cover duplicate timestamp, foreign-key order, and irreversible-operation diagnostics.
close_reason: RailsHx migration ordering, rollback-shape policy, and runtime-gate coverage implemented and covered by npm test.

title: Implement RailsHx reversible migration operations
description: Extend the typed RailsHx migration DSL with explicit reversible/up-down blocks, foreign-key operations, migration ordering checks, richer rollback-shape validation, Rails-native output, and positive/negative smoke coverage.
notes: Implemented typed reversible and foreign-key migration operations: MigrationOperation now includes ChangeColumn, AddForeignKey, RemoveForeignKey, ForeignKeyAction options, and Reversible(up, down). Compiler lowering emits Rails-native change_column, add_foreign_key/remove_foreign_key, and reversible do |dir| blocks from literal Haxe arrays. Todoapp sample/snapshots/smoke cover the output. Remaining migration ordering checks, broader rollback validation, and mandatory Rails db:migrate runtime execution are split into haxe.ruby-wpi.5.4.
close_reason: Typed reversible and foreign-key migration operations implemented and covered by npm test.

title: Implement initial RailsHx alter/drop/index migration operations
description: Add typed RailsHx migration operations beyond create-table-from-model metadata: alter table/add/remove/change columns, add/remove indexes and foreign keys, drop table, reversible/up-down shape where practical, Rails-native output, and positive/negative smoke coverage.
notes: Implemented first typed RailsHx migration operation lane: Haxe-authored operations:Array<MigrationOperation> lowers AddColumn/RemoveColumn/AddIndex/RemoveIndex/DropTable into Rails-native ActiveRecord change statements. Added todoapp sample coverage, generated snapshots, stdlib inventory/package coverage, and a negative smoke that rejects non-literal operation arguments. Remaining reversible/up-down, foreign-key operations, migration ordering, and richer rollback validation are split into a follow-up bead.
close_reason: Initial typed alter/drop/index migration operation DSL implemented and covered by npm test.

title: Validate RailsHx migration metadata
description: Add negative compile coverage for @:railsMigration metadata and tighten validation where needed: timestamp/className/models shape, unknown options, non-model references, duplicate migration outputs, duplicate model tables, and deterministic generated file names.
close_reason: Added RailsHx migration metadata registry validation and negative smoke coverage for duplicate tables, duplicate files, non-model refs, bad timestamps, and unknown options.

title: Support RailsHx through/source associations
description: Add typed RailsHx validation and emission for hasMany/hasOne through/source association options. Validate that through references a model-owned association where resolvable, source is literal and Rails-compatible, emit normal Rails association macros, and add positive/negative smoke coverage.
close_reason: Added typed through/source association option validation and Rails emission with positive and negative ActiveRecord smoke coverage.

title: Implement RailsHx typed ActiveRecord callbacks
description: Add typed RailsHx model lifecycle callback metadata/wrappers for Rails callbacks such as before_validation, before_save, after_create, and after_commit. Validate callback method references at compile time, emit idiomatic Rails callback macros, document escape-hatch policy, and cover positive/negative smoke tests.
close_reason: Added method-level RailsHx callback metadata validation, Rails callback emission, docs, and positive/negative ActiveRecord smoke coverage.

title: Support advanced RailsHx association options
description: Extend RailsHx association metadata beyond basic targets/FKs with typed Rails-native options such as dependent, optional, className, foreignKey, inverseOf, and through/source where appropriate. Validate resolvable model/field shapes, emit idiomatic Rails association macros, and add smoke coverage.
close_reason: Added typed association option validation/emission for optional, foreignKey, inverseOf, className, and dependent, with ActiveRecord smoke coverage and docs.

title: Emit typed RailsHx enum metadata
description: Add @:railsEnum model field metadata for Rails ActiveRecord enum declarations. Validate enum metadata at compile time, emit idiomatic Rails enum macros, update the ActiveRecord example, and cover invalid enum shapes in smoke tests.
close_reason: Added @:railsEnum compile-time metadata validation, Rails enum emission, ActiveRecord example coverage, and negative enum smoke tests.

title: Validate RailsHx validation option metadata
description: Add compile-time ModelMacro validation for @:validates option objects: allow Rails-native validation keys with literal/structured values, reject unknown keys and invalid value shapes, and cover diagnostics in ActiveRecord smoke tests.
close_reason: Added compile-time RailsHx validation target, option shape, unknown option, and Validation<T> checks with negative smoke coverage.

title: Validate RailsHx association metadata and foreign keys
description: Add compile-time ModelMacro validation for ActiveRecord association metadata: association fields must target model marker types when resolvable, belongsTo associations must have a matching railsColumn foreign key with compatible Int-like type, and diagnostics must be covered by negative smoke tests.
close_reason: Added compile-time RailsHx association target and belongsTo foreign-key validation with negative ActiveRecord smoke coverage.

title: Preserve RailsHx ActiveRecord criteria typing across Relation chains
description: Make Relation preserve the generated model criteria type across where/order/limit chains so assigned or widened relations still reject unknown fields and wrong value types. Keep Rails-native output and add negative smoke coverage after order/limit and typed variable assignment.
close_reason: Relation now preserves inferred criteria typing across where/order/limit chains, with assigned-relation negative coverage.

title: Make RailsHx runtime lanes explicit and mandatory in CI
description: Add an explicit RailsHx runtime test command/job that forces Rails integration and interop smoke tests to install/run Rails instead of silently skipping. Keep default npm test friendly for local compiler loops and document the Ruby/Rails setup.
close_reason: Added explicit RailsHx runtime command and mandatory CI job, with forced-mode bundle install behavior and docs.

title: Add RailsHx typed includes joins and scopes
description: Build on Relation<T> with typed association-aware includes/joins and model scopes. Use model/association metadata for compile-time validation and lower to normal ActiveRecord includes/joins/scope calls.
close_reason: Added typed ActiveRecord association refs plus includes/joins lowering and wrong-owner negative coverage.

title: Complete RailsHx Relation where/find/findBy typing
description: Extend the initial Relation<T> slice with relation-level typed where criteria plus typed find/findBy APIs. Preserve Rails-native output and add negative tests for unknown fields and wrong value types beyond model static where.
close_reason: Implemented typed model/relation where criteria plus typed find/findBy lowering and negative coverage.

title: Add RailsHx model fields/f aliases for typed field refs
description: Expose generated model field refs through Todo.fields.title and Todo.f.title while keeping Todo.titleField as a compatibility alias. Update todoapp/docs/tests to prefer the nested fields/f API.
close_reason: Added Todo.fields/Todo.f typed field-ref aliases, updated RailsHx todoapp/docs/tests, and kept generated Rails output native.

title: Make RailsHx template references compile-time checked
description: Replace unchecked RailsHx template path strings with compile-time checked template references where possible. Validate Template.named, Template.external path shape, @:railsTemplate paths, and layout names against RailsHx-owned template classes/generated artifacts or explicit external-template declarations.
acceptance_criteria: RailsHx-owned missing/renamed partial/template references fail at Haxe compile time; external templates remain explicit through Template.external and validate safe path shape; todoapp uses typed template/layout references where available; negative tests cover missing template, wrong locals, and unsafe path cases.
close_reason: Implemented checked RailsHx template references with Template.of/layout/existing, path validation, sample conversions, docs, generator update, and negative tests.

title: Make RailsHx form fields and strong params derive from typed model/schema fields
description: Remove duplicated string field names across RailsHx HHX form helpers and ParamsMacro.requirePermit by adding typed model/schema field references or checked field-name macros that lower to Rails snake_case strings/symbols.
acceptance_criteria: Todo form field names and strong params can be authored through typed model/schema field references; unknown fields fail at compile time; generated Rails output remains idiomatic field names and permitted params; negative tests cover unknown fields and wrong model scope.
close_reason: Implemented typed RailsHx ActiveRecord field refs/model params keys for HHX forms and ParamsMacro, with unknown-field and mixed-model negative coverage.

title: Implement RailsHx typed ActionView template layer
description: Build a typed Rails template layer inspired by ../haxe.elixir.codex's HXX/HEEx implementation. Provide Haxe-authored typed templates/components that lower to Rails-native Action View artifacts and helpers rather than leaving app views as untyped ERB islands.
acceptance_criteria: Design and implement a RailsHx template DSL/API with typed assigns/locals, typed route/form/helper access where feasible, compile-time validation for field/assign references, generated Rails-native templates or renderable Ruby components, strict escape-hatch policy for raw ERB, docs comparing the approach to HXX/HEEx, and a todoapp sample using the typed template path.
notes: 2026-06-11: Added typed external Rails template interop via Template.external(path):Template<TLocals>. ViewMacro and HHX partial/component lowering now accept Template.external alongside Template.named, preserving typed locals checks while emitting normal Rails render calls and not owning/emitting the external ERB artifact. Added rails_interop_app proving Haxe->legacy ERB, Haxe->legacy Ruby externs, legacy ERB->generated HHX partials, and legacy ERB->generated Haxe service calls. Verified npm run test:rails-interop, REQUIRE_RAILS=1 npm run test:rails-interop under rbenv Ruby 3.3.11, npm run test:todoapp-rails, npm run test:stdlib-inventory, REQUIRE_RAILS=1 npm run test:rails-integration, npm run test:strict-boundaries, ci:version-sync, and ci:release-contracts.
close_reason: Closed the initial RailsHx typed ActionView layer: HHX-first @:railsTemplateAst templates, typed HtmlNode/HtmlAttr/H lower-level AST, typed locals projection, Template.of/layout/existing/external references, checked owned/external template paths, typed partial/component/slot composition, route/link/form helper tags, raw ERB guarded by @:railsAllowRawErb, todoapp coverage, rails_interop_app gradual-adoption coverage, docs, negative tests, snapshots, and package/inventory coverage are in place. Remaining broader ergonomics/framework expansion belongs to follow-up RailsHx tasks rather than this initial layer parent.

title: Add RailsHx end-to-end Rails integration app and CI gate
description: Create a RailsHx integration app/test gate that proves Haxe-authored Rails models, migrations, controllers, params, routes, and generated Ruby run in Rails. Mirror haxe.elixir.codex's end-to-end Phoenix/Ecto examples and sentinel tests, adapted to Rails db:migrate and rails test.
acceptance_criteria: CI can compile the Haxe Rails app, materialize generated Ruby, run Rails migrations, and execute Rails integration tests when Rails gems are available or REQUIRE_RAILS=1; local skips remain explicit; failures identify compiler vs Rails runtime stage.
notes: Hardened RailsHx Playwright browser gate: added dedicated GitHub Actions RailsHx browser sentinel job, release-contract assertions for the job/script, runner port conflict handling/free-port fallback, Chromium cache detection, signal cleanup/server-log reporting, and UX regression assertions for broken links/back-to-todos artifacts plus todo-dot containment. Verified npm test and npm run test:todoapp-playwright pass.
close_reason: Completed RailsHx end-to-end integration gate: todoapp Rails compile/materialization, generated Ruby syntax checks, optional Rails db:migrate/rails test runtime with explicit REQUIRE_RAILS behavior, mixed Rails/RailsHx interop runtime lane, Playwright browser sentinel, CI/release-contract assertions, and failure messages separating compiler, generated app, and Rails runtime stages.

title: Implement RailsHx route helper sync and later Haxe-first routing DSL
description: Harden the existing rails routes parser into a typed route helper sync, then design an optional Haxe-first routes DSL inspired by haxe.elixir.codex's Phoenix RouterDsl. Start with generated extern helpers from Rails-owned config/routes.rb; only add Haxe-owned route emission after helper sync is deterministic and tested.
acceptance_criteria: rails routes output generates typed path/url externs with required path params; helpers preserve Rails names while exposing Haxe-friendly method names; parser handles resources, member/collection routes, namespaces, nested params, and engines where feasible; docs mark Haxe-first route emission as phase 2.
close_reason: Hardened RailsHx route helper sync from Rails-owned rails routes output. Parser now handles carried resource prefixes, namespaces, member/collection helpers, nested resource params, multi-verb routes, optional segments, glob params, duplicate helper prefixes, mount-like rows, and internal rails_* skips. Added complex fixture coverage and docs clarifying Haxe-owned route emission remains deferred.

title: Implement RailsHx typed controllers, params, and action results
description: Expand the current ActionController Base/ParamsMacro seed into typed Rails controller surfaces inspired by haxe.elixir.codex's Phoenix controller/socket/assign abstractions. Cover strong params from typedefs/model schemas, render/redirect/head result shapes, session, flash, cookies, request, response, filters, and narrow dynamic boundaries.
acceptance_criteria: Controller APIs use typed Haxe params/results while lowering to Rails ActionController idioms; ParamsMacro supports typedef-driven permit lists with snake_case lowering; tests cover required/optional params, nested params, render/json/redirect/head, filters, flash/session/cookies, and invalid permit fields.
notes: 2026-06-13: Added typed nested strong-params permit specs. ParamsMacro.requirePermit now accepts an optional fourth Rails-shaped object literal for nested specs, e.g. ParamsMacro.requirePermit(params, root, [Todo.f.title], {metadata: ["source"], tags: []}), while preserving existing three-arg calls. Added rails.action_controller.PermitSpec as a typed std facade; macro output uses PermitSpec.field/nested and compiler lowering emits Rails-native permit arrays with symbols and nested hashes, avoiding raw __ruby__ so strict-boundary checks pass. action_controller_params fixture/smoke and snapshots cover permit([:title, :is_completed, {metadata: [:source, :priority]}, {tags: []}]). Updated compatibility matrix, stdlib inventory, gap report, and haxelib package list. Verified npm run test:action-controller-params, UPDATE_SNAPSHOTS=1 npm run test:snapshots, UPDATE_GAP_REPORT=1 npm run test:gap-report, npm run test:gap-report, npm run test:stdlib-inventory, npm run test:haxelib-package, npm test.
close_reason: Completed typed RailsHx controller/params/action-result surface: typed field and nested strong params, render/json/redirect/head status lowering, flash/session/cookies stores, request/response facades, method-backed filters, and smoke/snapshot/negative coverage.

title: Implement RailsHx typed migration DSL and generator
description: Build Haxe-authored Rails migrations inspired by haxe.elixir.codex's Ecto Migration, MigrationBuilder, MigrationRegistry, and MigrationValidator. Emit standard ActiveRecord::Migration Ruby files while validating tables, columns, indexes, foreign keys, reversible operations, timestamps, and migration ordering where practical.
acceptance_criteria: A Haxe migration DSL emits timestamped Rails migration files; invalid table/column/index references fail or warn by documented policy; generated migrations run with rails db:migrate in the integration app; tests cover create/alter/drop tables, indexes, foreign keys, timestamps, rollback shape, and deterministic file names.
notes: Implemented initial RailsHx migration artifact slice: Haxe-authored @:railsMigration classes emit timestamped ActiveRecord create-table migrations from referenced @:railsModel/@:railsColumn metadata, including timestamps, defaults/nullability, indexes, and belongs_to references. Todoapp and scaffold generator now use Haxe migration source; generated db/migrate/*.rb is compiler output. Remaining work: richer typed migration DSL for alter/drop/reversible operations, validation, ordering, rollback shape, and broader negative tests.
close_reason: Completed typed RailsHx migration DSL/generator: @:railsMigration emits timestamped ActiveRecord migration files; MigrationOperation covers create-derived metadata plus add/remove/change columns, indexes, foreign keys, reversible blocks, and drop table; validation covers metadata shape, duplicate timestamps/files, table/column references, external tables, ordering of FK targets, rollback/destructive operation policy, deterministic file names, syntax checks, and optional Rails db:migrate runtime lane when local Rails gems are available.

title: Implement RailsHx typed associations, validations, enums, and callbacks
description: Extend ModelMacro/registry beyond metadata validation into typed ActiveRecord relationship and lifecycle surfaces. Use haxe.elixir.codex's Ecto association validator as inspiration for compile-time model shape validation, adapted to Rails association names, foreign keys, dependent options, validations, enums, and callbacks.
acceptance_criteria: Belongs-to/has-many/has-one/through metadata validates target model and FK shape when resolvable; Rails validations/enums/callbacks have typed wrappers; generated Ruby emits idiomatic Rails macros; warnings/errors have strict and escape-hatch policy documented and tested.
close_reason: Completed RailsHx typed associations, validations, enums, callbacks, advanced options, and through/source coverage across child tasks.

title: Implement RailsHx typed ActiveRecord relation and query DSL
description: Add a typed ActiveRecord query layer inspired by haxe.elixir.codex's Ecto TypedQuery. Provide Relation<T> and typed helpers for where, find, findBy, order, limit, includes, joins, scopes, and chaining while lowering to idiomatic Rails ActiveRecord calls.
acceptance_criteria: Object-literal query keys are checked against model columns/associations; value types are checked; Relation<T> chains preserve model type; generated Ruby uses Rails-native query methods; negative tests cover unknown fields and wrong value types.
notes: 2026-06-13: Added typed ActiveRecord exists/count slice. ModelMacro now generates Todo.exists(?criteria) -> Bool and Todo.count() -> Int stubs; Relation<T, TCriteria> exposes exists(?criteria) and count(); compiler lowers exists/exists? criteria through the same snake_case criteria path as where/findBy and emits Rails-native exists?. ActiveRecord example/docs/smoke cover model/relation exists?, count(), and bad exists criteria. Verified npm run test:active-record-model, npm run test:todoapp-rails, npm run test:rails-integration, npm run test:stdlib-inventory, UPDATE_SNAPSHOTS=1 npm run test:snapshots, npm test.
close_reason: Completed typed ActiveRecord query layer: typed criteria object keys/value types, typed model-owned field and association refs, Relation<T, TCriteria> chain preservation, find/findBy/exists/count/order/limit/includes/joins/toArray lowering to Rails-native ActiveRecord calls, static scope pattern, and negative tests for unknown fields, wrong value types, bad association owner, and bad findBy/exists criteria.

title: Implement RailsHx ActiveRecord schema registry
description: Expand the current rails.active_record.Base<T> and ModelMacro seed into a schema metadata registry inspired by haxe.elixir.codex's Ecto SchemaRegistrar. Support typed columns, inferred/default table names, nullability, defaults, timestamps, indexes, primary keys, and Rails-friendly snake_case lowering.
acceptance_criteria: Model metadata is collected at compile time; invalid column metadata fails compilation; generated Ruby model classes preserve Rails ApplicationRecord shape; tests cover table inference, explicit table names, columns, nullability, defaults, timestamps, and invalid metadata.
notes: 2026-06-10: Implemented RailsHx ActiveRecord schema-registry slice. Added typed ColumnOptions<T>, railsColumn option validation, generated Model.__hx_rails_schema metadata, explicit/inferred table fixtures, invalid defaultValue compile check, docs/inventory/matrix updates, and refreshed snapshots. Completed Rails superclass policy by emitting models as ::ApplicationRecord and materializing app/models/application_record.rb in the integration harness; npm test passes.
close_reason: Implemented ActiveRecord schema registry metadata with validated typed columns and Rails-native ::ApplicationRecord superclass output; npm test passes.

title: Document RailsHx architecture contract
description: Write the canonical RailsHx architecture contract using ../haxe.elixir.codex Phoenix/Ecto as the implementation inspiration. Cover one Ruby compiler pipeline, ruby_first Rails-first default, portable domain code support, no metal profile, framework-owned typed std surfaces, macro registries/builders, generators, and Rails integration tests.
acceptance_criteria: Docs explain how RailsHx maps the haxe.elixir.codex pattern to Rails/ActiveRecord; AGENTS.md tells future agents to inspect ../haxe.elixir.codex Phoenix/Ecto implementation before designing RailsHx features; docs clearly distinguish current Rails MVP from target RailsHx.
close_reason: RailsHx roadmap doc and AGENTS guidance added; README and compatibility matrix now distinguish Rails MVP from RailsHx roadmap.

title: RailsHx typed Rails compiler
description: Promote the current Rails MVP into a first-class RailsHx layer comparable in shape to haxe.elixir's Phoenix/Ecto integration: typed Rails std APIs, metadata registries, macro lowering, generators, docs, and real Rails integration tests. This remains one Ruby compiler pipeline using the ruby_first Rails-first contract, not a separate backend or metal profile.
acceptance_criteria: Open child tasks cover architecture/docs, ActiveRecord schema/query/associations/validations, migrations, controllers/params, routes, generators, integration tests, and framework docs.
close_reason: Completed RailsHx typed Rails compiler epic. Closed child tasks cover architecture/profile contract, ActiveRecord schema/query/associations/validations/enums/callbacks, typed migrations, controllers/params/action results, route helper sync, HHX/ActionView templates, generators/adoption tooling, end-to-end Rails integration and Playwright gates, API docs, and post-core Rails surface planning. RailsHx remains one ruby_first/portable compiler pipeline with Rails-native generated Ruby and no Ruby metal profile.

acceptance_criteria: hello_world compiles through reflaxe.ruby using -lib reflaxe.ruby or equivalent local classpaths; generated Ruby is executed with ruby; stdout matches the committed fixture expectation.
close_reason: Updated hello_world smoke to compile through reflaxe.ruby bootstrap/init using available vendored Reflaxe classpaths, execute generated Main.rb with ruby, and compare stdout; npm test passes.
