title: R&D HHX view-local helper methods with GPT-5.5 Pro
description: Research and design first-class helper methods inside @:railsTemplateAst RailsHx view classes. Goal: let pure presentation helpers live with HHX views, callable from markup, without turning views into controller/query/mutation objects. Explore static/private vs instance-style surfaces, macro lowering, generated ERB/Ruby shape, diagnostics, tests, and whether this can reduce separate viewmodel/helper boilerplate while staying Rails-native and beginner-friendly.
design:
# RailsHx View-Local Helpers R&D RailsHx HHX views should be allowed to keep small, pure presentation helpers next to the markup that uses them. This is the same ergonomic pressure React components solve with local functions, but the RailsHx output must remain normal Rails ERB and must not turn view classes into query, mutation, or controller objects. This document is the design packet for the `haxe_ruby-7yo` R&D bead. It is written so a beginner can understand the intended authoring model before reading the compiler implementation. ## Problem Today, a RailsHx-owned template is usually a static view class: ```haxe @:railsTemplate("todos/_card") @:railsTemplateAst("render") class TodoCardView { public static function render(locals:TodoCardLocals):HtmlNode { return

${locals.todo.title}

; } } ``` That is good for type safety, but view files quickly need tiny formatting choices: - choose a CSS class from a model state; - format a short label; - choose empty-state copy; - normalize display-only text; - keep repeated HHX fragments readable. Without a first-class rule, authors either push those decisions into extra view-model objects, duplicate expressions inline, or add ad hoc helper classes that are far away from the markup. That is unnecessary ceremony for pure presentation logic. ## Goals - Let view authors define pure helper methods in the same class as the HHX template. - Keep helper calls typed by Haxe and discoverable in editors. - Emit boring Rails ERB/Ruby, with no custom view-helper runtime. - Make the boundary obvious: helpers may format, branch, compose local markup, and call typed RailsHx view/helper facades; helpers must not query databases, mutate records, touch sessions, enqueue jobs, or perform controller work. - Reduce view-model/helper boilerplate when the logic is genuinely local to one view. - Preserve the current `@:railsTemplateAst("render")` authoring path and make this an additive feature. ## Non-Goals - Do not introduce stateful view component instances in the first slice. - Do not replace Rails helpers, Rails partials, or typed RailsHx components. - Do not allow view helpers to become service objects. - Do not add hidden compiler globals. - Do not emit helper methods into app-facing Rails classes unless that output is necessary and clearly documented. ## Recommended Authoring Shape The first supported shape should be static/private helper methods in the same view class: ```haxe typedef TodoCardLocals = { var todo:Todo; } @:railsTemplate("todos/_card") @:railsTemplateAst("render") class TodoCardView { public static function render(locals:TodoCardLocals):HtmlNode { return

${titleText(locals.todo)}

${statusLabel(locals.todo)}
; } static function cardClass(todo:Todo):String { return todo.isCompleted ? "todo-card is-complete" : "todo-card"; } static function titleText(todo:Todo):String { return todo.title.trim(); } static function statusLabel(todo:Todo):String { return todo.isCompleted ? "Done" : "Open"; } } ``` Beginners only need to learn one rule: if the helper is just about displaying this template, put it in the view class; if it loads data or changes state, keep it in the controller/model/service layer. ## Helper Kinds ### String Helpers String, number, bool, enum, and date-ish helpers are the safest first slice. They can appear in text or attributes: ```haxe ${displayName(locals.user)} ``` Desired ERB shape: ```erb <%= display_name(user) %> ``` For RailsHx-owned static helpers, the compiler may also inline very small pure expressions when that keeps output clearer, but direct ERB helper calls are easier to debug and should be the initial contract. ### Markup Helpers A helper that returns `HtmlNode` can keep repeated HHX fragments close to the view: ```haxe static function badge(text:String):HtmlNode { return ${text}; } ``` Desired lowering: ```haxe ${badge("New")} ``` should emit the helper's markup at the call site when all arguments can be lowered through HHX. This is more compiler work than string helpers, so it should be a second slice after simple expression helpers. ### Shared Helpers If a helper becomes useful across multiple views, move it to a typed shared facade: ```haxe import views.helpers.UserDisplay.displayName; ``` Shared helpers should follow the same purity rule and can later become generated Rails helpers only when Ruby/Rails interop makes that the clearest output. ## Compiler Contract The implementation should add an explicit RailsHx view-helper contract instead of relying on incidental expression printing. 1. When lowering `@:railsTemplateAst`, collect static methods declared on the view class. 2. Treat calls from `render(...)` to same-class static helpers as view-local helper calls. 3. Require helper arguments and return types to be known. `Dynamic` should fail unless a deliberately named escape hatch exists. 4. Allow only helper return types the template lowerer understands: `String`, `Bool`, `Int`, `Float`, Rails-safe scalar abstracts, and later `HtmlNode`. 5. Reject or warn on helpers that touch obvious non-view surfaces such as `ActiveRecord` query methods, controller stores, session, cookies, jobs, file IO, raw Ruby, or `untyped`. 6. Emit Rails-native ERB: - scalar helpers lower to a Ruby method call or inlined expression; - markup helpers lower to HHX output at the call site or a small generated private ERB helper only if inlining would duplicate complex markup. 7. If generated support is needed, isolate it under the same generated support policy as other RailsHx output and add a generated comment explaining why it exists. ## Naming Haxe authors should use Haxe names: ```haxe static function displayName(user:User):String ``` Generated Ruby should use normal Ruby names: ```ruby def display_name(user) ``` If the helper is inlined, no Ruby method is emitted. If the helper is emitted, it should live as close as Rails convention allows, for example as a private helper method associated with the generated template support, not as a random `__hx_*` method on a model or controller. ## Diagnostics Good beginner diagnostics matter more than clever lowering. Examples: - `TodoCardView.cardClass returns Dynamic; view-local helpers must return a known display type such as String or HtmlNode.` - `TodoCardView.loadTodos calls Todo.where(...). View-local helpers must not query the database; load data in the controller and pass it through typed locals.` - `TodoCardView.saveLabel mutates locals.todo. View-local helpers must be pure presentation logic.` - `badge(...) returns HtmlNode, but markup-returning helpers are not enabled yet. Use a partial/component or inline HHX for now.` ## Tests The implementation should include: - a positive HHX template smoke for same-class scalar helpers in text and attrs; - a positive snapshot proving generated ERB/Ruby has readable helper names and no `__hx*` scaffolding; - a compile-fail test for `Dynamic` helper returns; - a compile-fail test for database/query calls inside a helper; - a compile-fail test for mutations inside a helper; - a future positive test for `HtmlNode` helper composition; - todoapp coverage once the API is ready, because the todoapp is the canonical dogfood app for RailsHx ergonomics. ## Relationship To View Models View-local helpers do not replace view models completely. Use this rule: - If the value is derived from already-loaded locals and only affects this template, use a view-local helper. - If the value is shared across views, move it to a shared typed helper. - If the value represents a larger presentation contract, use a typed locals typedef or view model. - If the value requires loading data, permissions, persistence, or side effects, compute it before rendering and pass it as typed locals. ## Implementation Slices 1. Document and test the current behavior around simple helper calls, so we know which cases already work by accident. 2. Add explicit same-class scalar helper recognition in template expression printing. 3. Add purity diagnostics for the most dangerous surfaces: queries, mutation, `untyped`, raw Ruby, controller/session/cookie/job/file IO calls. 4. Add todoapp examples where helpers remove noisy repeated presentation logic. 5. Add `HtmlNode` returning helpers after scalar helpers are stable. 6. Revisit an instance-style surface only after static helpers are proven: ```haxe class TodoCardView extends RailsView { function cardClass(todo:Todo):String; public function render():HtmlNode; } ``` This may be nicer someday, but it is more magic and should not be the first contract. ## Review Packet For A Future GPT-5.5 Pro Pass Ask the reviewer to challenge these points: - Is the static helper surface enough, or should the first public API reserve syntax for an eventual instance-style view class? - Should scalar helpers emit Ruby helper methods or inline their bodies by default? - What purity checks are practical in Haxe typed AST without rejecting useful presentation code? - Is `HtmlNode` helper inlining the right second slice, or should repeated markup always become typed partials/components? - Which examples in `examples/todoapp_rails/src/views` would most clearly demonstrate the feature without making the sample clever? ## Recommendation Implement static same-class scalar helpers first. It is the smallest feature that gives authors the React-like local-function ergonomics they want, keeps output Rails-native, and creates a clear place for diagnostics. Treat `HtmlNode` helper composition and instance-style views as follow-up R&D after the scalar helper contract is tested in the todoapp.
notes: Completed R&D design draft for HHX view-local helpers. No external GPT-5.5 Pro reviewer is available from this session, so the doc includes a dedicated future review packet. Implementation has been split into haxe_ruby-7pt with the design doc as its spec.

title: Add ActionMailer preview and test-helper generator UX
description: Design and implement the generator/user workflow around typed parameterized mailers and previews. The generated Rails app skeleton should make the supported mailer dev loop obvious without replacing Rails' ActionMailer runtime or preview server.
acceptance_criteria: Docs and generator help show how to create a typed mailer, parameterized delivery wrapper, preview class, and Rails test; any generator-created files are Haxe/HHX source or manifest-owned Rails artifacts; action-mailer runtime tests cover the generated workflow under REQUIRE_RAILS=1; unsupported preview/test-helper shapes are linked to follow-up beads with rationale.
close_reason: Implemented ActionMailer generator UX: added hxruby:mailer Rails generator, repository script, Rake hxruby:gen:mailer task, Haxe-owned mailer/HHX preview/test skeleton generation, generator smoke coverage including generated-Haxe compile and Ruby syntax checks, gem package coverage, and docs/README/compatibility updates.

title: Add examples compile QA gate
description: Add a CI task that compiles every example entrypoint so examples stay relevant after compiler changes. Examples that demonstrate runtime/framework behavior should also have focused smoke/runtime/browser coverage; compile-only is acceptable only when the compiler output/snapshot already owns the contract.
close_reason: Implemented examples QA gate: npm run test:examples-compile compiles every examples/*/Main.hx plus the todoapp client build and audits each example has declared snapshot/smoke/runtime/browser coverage. Wired into npm test and release-contract checks; documented in AGENTS and testing strategy.

title: Dogfood serious Ruby tooling with Haxe-authored Ruby
description: Design and implement a representative Haxe-authored Ruby tooling slice that consumes real Ruby APIs such as Ripper/Bundler/Rake/Rails where appropriate, compiles to idiomatic Ruby, and is used behind a Ruby-native adapter. This proves haxe.ruby can author serious Ruby tooling, not only application code, while preserving Ruby/Rails-native UX for host integration seams.
acceptance_criteria: At least one stable reusable tooling core is authored in Haxe and compiled to Ruby; it consumes/represents real Ruby APIs through typed externs/contracts where needed; Ruby-native adapters remain where Rails expects them; snapshots show the generated Ruby is readable and idiomatic; existing smoke/runtime behavior is preserved; docs explain when to choose Ruby-native vs Haxe-authored Ruby.
close_reason: Implemented the first serious Haxe-authored Ruby tooling dogfood slice in route parity. The Ruby/Rails adapter remains Ruby-native for OptionParser/stdin/task integration, while tools/route_parity_hx now owns JSON/file-backed manifest loading, Devise mapping facts validation, and route parity comparison in Haxe. Added typed ruby.Json and ruby.File externs, regenerated committed route parity Ruby snapshots, added dogfood checks for JSON.parse/File.read output, documented the Ruby-native adapter vs Haxe-authored core boundary, and updated std inventory. Verified npm run test:routes-generator, npm run test:stdlib-inventory, npm run test:haxelib-package, npm run ci:release-contracts, and git diff --check.

title: Write RailsHx skeleton and todoapp tutorial guide
description: Add a tutorial-style guide for the generated RailsHx skeleton/template and the todoapp dogfood app. Explain the development workflow, source-of-truth model, how RailsHx differs from vanilla Rails in positive/productive ways, and the patterns used in the todoapp.
acceptance_criteria: Docs cover starting from the RailsHx skeleton, editing Haxe controllers/models/routes/HHX views, running watcher/server/test flows, understanding generated Rails artifacts, using typed HHX views instead of hand-written ERB, using DeviseHx/current-user/topbar patterns, typed params/model fields/routes/templates, and when to keep Rails-owned files for adoption. README/docs link to the tutorial.
close_reason: accepted: added docs/railshx-skeleton-and-todoapp-tutorial.md, linked it from docs and the todoapp README, and documented the RailsHx skeleton/todoapp workflow, source-of-truth model, HHX views, DeviseHx/current-user/topbar patterns, typed params/model fields/routes/templates, tests, and adoption boundaries.

title: Investigate RailsHx textarea Enter-submit browser shortcut
description: Playwright DeviseHx/todoapp coverage found that the typed rails.hotwire.TextAreaComposer helper and pre-submit textarea keyboard interactions need isolation. Shift+Enter newline setup followed by submit made the single-page chat post spec unreliable, while the normal visible Rails submit button path works in the two-browser Turbo Stream spec. Investigate whether this is Playwright key dispatch, modifier state, Turbo timing, form focus, or the helper implementation; keep the fix Rails/Hotwire-native with no artificial delays. Add/restore browser assertions for Enter submits and Shift+Enter newline preservation once the behavior is understood.
close_reason: Replaced the custom textarea keyup coordination with a vanilla Hotwire-shaped keydown handler that calls the typed Forms.requestSubmit facade immediately for plain Enter, leaves Shift+Enter to browser newline behavior, ignores composing/repeat events, and added Playwright coverage proving Enter submits and Shift+Enter preserves multiline input. Verified npm run test:todoapp-rails and npm run test:todoapp-playwright.

title: Add strict Devise currentRequired flow diagnostics
description: Add strict-mode compile diagnostics for DeviseHx currentRequired usage. The compiler should verify currentRequired(this, UserAuth.scope) or generated UserAuth.currentRequired(this) is only used in actions protected by the matching beforeAction(UserAuth.authenticate), or require an explicit runtime assertion/unsafe boundary. This should understand lifecycle only/except options and report source-positioned diagnostics for unguarded actions and wrong-scope guards.
acceptance_criteria: A strict DeviseHx define enables flow diagnostics; protected actions with matching beforeAction(UserAuth.authenticate) compile; unprotected actions using currentRequired fail with a useful diagnostic; actions protected by a different scope fail; only/except lifecycle options are respected; tests cover direct Auth.currentRequired and generated UserAuth.currentRequired wrappers.
close_reason: Implemented opt-in strict Devise currentRequired flow diagnostics with lifecycle only/except and scope-specific guard checks, plus controller smoke coverage and docs.

title: Add Devise mapping facts probe and route manifest v2 parity hardening
description: Harden Haxe-owned Devise route parity after the no-options DeviseRoutes.deviseFor MVP. Add a Rails/Devise facts probe that boots a fresh Rails process, reads Devise.mappings, validates mapping identity from .railshx/routes.haxe.json, and keeps route helper extern generation based only on actual rails routes output. Introduce route manifest versioning/unknown-kind fatal behavior without guessing Devise helper matrices.
acceptance_criteria: A Devise manifest entry triggers a fresh Devise.mappings facts probe; parity compares mapping name, className, path/scoped path, and model-devise presence; rails routes output is captured once and reused for route extern generation/parity; unknown route manifest versions and unknown declaration kinds fail loudly; no helper matrix is reimplemented in Haxe/Ruby parity code; tests cover missing mapping, wrong class/path, rails routes failure, malformed facts, and unchanged Rails-owned route sync.
close_reason: Implemented Devise mapping facts parity: hxruby:routes now captures rails routes with checked status, probes Devise.mappings for Haxe-owned deviseFor manifests, validates mapping name/class/path/scopedPath/modelHasDevise, writes route externs only after parity passes, and route parity now fails on unsupported manifest versions/unknown kinds. Covered by routes-generator/routes-dsl/todoapp-rails gates.

title: Add typed Devise route options for only/skip and split mappings
description: Extend DeviseRoutes.deviseFor beyond the no-options MVP with a typed options object for Devise route shaping. Support literal, normalized only/skip route-group selection and split mappings without reproducing Devise's helper matrix; emit ordinary devise_for options, keep Rails/Devise as the route expansion oracle, and validate helper externs from actual rails routes output.
acceptance_criteria: DeviseRoutes.deviseFor(UserAuth.scope, {only: [...]}) and {skip: [...]} use typed Devise route-group tokens; only and skip cannot be combined; duplicate mapping scopes are allowed only for supported split mappings with compatible only/skip semantics; generated config/routes.rb remains idiomatic devise_for; route manifest records semantic options; parity still validates Devise mapping facts and consumes actual Rails route output rather than a guessed helper matrix; docs explain Rails-owned fallback for unsupported Devise route options.
close_reason: Implemented typed Devise only/skip route options and split mappings: added DeviseRouteGroup tokens, macro validation for object-literal only/skip arrays, Rails route IR/emitter/manifest support, duplicate split mapping validation, docs, package inclusion, and route DSL smoke coverage. Advanced Devise route options remain Rails-owned until separate typed contracts land.

title: Harden Devise adoption route-authorability metadata
description: Replace the current conservative regex route-authorability inference with Ripper-backed Devise route declaration analysis before broader Devise route options are supported. Mark generated scope metadata routeAuthorable=true only for plain top-level devise_for with one literal resource and no options/block/surrounding scope; generate routeAuthorable=false plus a reason for Rails-owned complex mappings.
acceptance_criteria: Plain devise_for :users generates authorable route metadata; class_name/singular/path/controllers/options/block/nested scope cases generate non-authorable metadata with diagnostics; DeviseRoutes.deviseFor on a non-authorable contract fails with the stored reason; tests cover both authorable and non-authorable adoption.
close_reason: Replaced regex Devise route adoption with conservative Ripper-backed analysis, added route_authorable metadata/reasons/diagnostics, generated non-authorable contract reasons, and covered authorable/non-authorable adoption plus DeviseRoutes failure behavior in focused smoke tests.

title: Document reusable RailsHx gem-layer testing pyramid
description: Capture the standard testing pattern for typed RailsHx companion gem layers: deterministic inventory/adoption smoke for fail-closed behavior, committed snapshots for generated contracts/docs/JSON, Haxe compile checks for generated app-local contracts, and focused dogfood app request/Playwright flows only where Rails must consume runtime-facing artifacts. Apply the pattern to DeviseHx docs and future gem-layer templates.
acceptance_criteria: Docs describe the reusable gem-layer test pyramid; DeviseHx docs link to it; future gem adoption beads can point at the rule instead of rediscovering test scope.
close_reason: accepted: added docs/railshx-gem-layer-testing.md and linked it from the docs index, gem-layer guide, and DeviseHx design. The guide captures the companion-gem testing pyramid: deterministic inventory/generator smoke, generated artifact snapshots, Haxe compile/negative tests, thin Rails runtime seams, and focused dogfood browser flows without retesting the Ruby gem runtime.

title: Expand todoapp with DeviseHx dogfood auth
description: Use the canonical RailsHx todoapp as the first integrated DeviseHx dogfood slice. Demonstrate normal Devise runtime ownership with Haxe/HHX-owned RailsHx app code, typed DeviseHx contracts, Haxe-owned devise_for routing, protected controllers, guest login, and polished auth UX.
acceptance_criteria: examples/todoapp_rails includes an app-local UserAuth DeviseHx contract with route metadata; the User model emits normal devise :... Ruby from typed Haxe metadata; Haxe-owned routes use DeviseRoutes.deviseFor(UserAuth.scope) plus a typed guest sign-in route; controllers use beforeAction(UserAuth.authenticate) and current/currentRequired helpers; the HHX auth panel documents and demonstrates login, logout, and continue-as-guest flows; Rails request/model/route tests and Playwright cover public guest gate, guest login, protected access, sign out, todo creation, user management, and Turbo chat; docs explain the DeviseHx slice and future dedicated DeviseHx app/package hardening remains tracked separately.
notes: Added a user-management hardening slice: the admin Remove user action now uses the same typed RailsHx form_with method=delete path as create/update instead of a mixed button_to branch, the todoapp smoke asserts the generated DELETE form output, and Playwright now covers styled Devise login/guest/logout controls, logout/protected-route behavior, guest/non-admin denial for /users, admin CRUD delete, styled admin buttons, and hiding self-delete for the current admin. Verified with npm run test:todoapp-rails, npm run test:todoapp-playwright, and git diff --check.
close_reason: Expanded todoapp with DeviseHx dogfood auth: UserAuth contract, typed Devise model metadata, Haxe-owned deviseFor routing, guest sign-in, protected controllers, polished login/topbar UX, admin-only user CRUD, session-aware todos/chat, Rails request/model/route tests, and Playwright coverage for login/logout/guest/admin/user management/Turbo chat. Verified npm run test:todoapp-rails and npm run test:todoapp-playwright.

title: Add DeviseHx HHX helpers and view generator
description: Add typed HHX helpers and optional generated HHX views for Devise sessions, registrations, passwords, confirmations, unlocks, flash, errors, and auth navigation.
acceptance_criteria: Typed sign-in/sign-out/sign-up/edit-registration helpers compile; sign-out defaults to Turbo-safe button/form semantics; generated Devise HHX views use railsTemplateAst and never raw ERB as source; existing ERB views are adopted through checked contracts only; snapshots and Rails view/runtime tests cover generated helpers and ownership checks.
notes: Eighth implementation slice complete: hardened DeviseHx HHX view adoption ownership tests. rails-adopt-generator smoke now proves --devise-hhx-views can regenerate manifest-owned Devise HHX skeletons and refuses to overwrite a hand-written/non-RailsHx-owned SessionsNewView.hx. This completes the acceptance criteria for the DeviseHx HHX helper/view-generator slice, but bd refuses to close haxe.ruby-bjv.6.4.7 while dependency haxe.ruby-bjv.3.4 remains open, so leave it in progress instead of force-closing. Verified with npm run test:rails-adopt-generator and git diff --check.
close_reason: Implemented DeviseHx HHX helpers and view generator: typed auth links/forms/errors compile through HHX, sign-out uses Turbo-safe button/form semantics, generated Devise views are Haxe/HHX source with railsTemplateAst, non-owned view overwrites fail closed, and adoption generator coverage proves manifest-owned regeneration. Verified npm run test:rails-adopt-generator.

title: Add Devise model module and schema validation
description: Represent known Devise model modules as typed specs and validate conventional schema requirements for Haxe-owned and Rails-owned adopted models.
acceptance_criteria: Known modules database_authenticatable/registerable/recoverable/rememberable/validatable/confirmable/lockable/trackable/timeoutable/omniauthable have typed specs/options; Haxe-owned models emit normal devise :... Ruby; Rails-owned model externs are generated with review markers; schema checks fail closed for missing required columns; negative tests cover missing columns, unknown modules, and explicit unsafeCustom usage.
notes: Additional hardening slice complete: rails-adopt-generator smoke now includes module-specific Devise schema failure coverage for Rails-owned adoption. Fixtures with :confirmable but no confirmation columns fail on confirmation_token, and fixtures with :lockable but no lock columns fail on failed_attempts. This complements the existing Haxe-owned @:devise schema validation and generic missing-schema tests. Verified with npm run test:rails-adopt-generator and git diff --check. The bead remains in_progress only because bd blocks closure on the broader haxe.ruby-bjv.3.2 dependency.
close_reason: Implemented Devise model module and schema validation: known Devise module specs/options are typed, Haxe-owned models emit normal devise :... Ruby, Rails-owned adoption validates module schema requirements with review markers, and negative coverage catches missing confirmable/lockable columns plus unknown/unsafe custom modules. Verified npm run test:rails-adopt-generator.

title: Add Devise controller lifecycle and current-user typing
description: Implement typed Devise controller helper integration: lifecycle auth filters, nullable/currentRequired current-user APIs, signed-in checks, sign-in/sign-out helpers, and custom Devise controller hook signatures.
acceptance_criteria: beforeAction(UserAuth.authenticate) lowers to before_action :authenticate_user!; current/currentRequired/signedIn/signIn/signOut/bypassSignIn compile and lower to normal Devise helpers; wrong-scope and unguarded currentRequired strict-mode diagnostics are covered; Rails request tests prove protected page, login, logout, and signed-in access.
notes: Implemented the compiler/generator/docs slice: generated UserAuth contracts now carry @:deviseHxAuthFilter/@:deviseHxHelper metadata; beforeAction(UserAuth.authenticate) lowers to before_action :authenticate_user!; generated UserAuth.current/currentRequired/signedIn/signIn/signOut and low-level Auth.current/currentRequired/signedIn/signIn/bypassSignIn/signOut/signOutAll lower to normal Devise helpers; focused devisehx-controller smoke asserts generated Ruby. Todoapp now also proves protected page, login, logout, guest session, and signed-in access through Rails request tests and Playwright. Remaining acceptance is split/tracked in haxe.ruby-9a6 for strict currentRequired flow diagnostics.
close_reason: Completed Devise controller lifecycle/current-user typing: beforeAction(UserAuth.authenticate) lowers to authenticate_user!, current/currentRequired/signedIn/signIn/bypassSignIn/signOut lower to normal Devise helpers, strict currentRequired diagnostics are covered by haxe.ruby-9a6, and focused controller smoke is green.

title: Implement deterministic Devise extractor and adoption generator
description: Add Devise-specific deterministic discovery on top of the generic --gem adoption lane. Inventory Bundler specs, Devise initializer, routes, models, schema, custom controllers/mailers, and RailsHx ownership metadata without executing arbitrary app code.
acceptance_criteria: A Devise adoption command or --devise-contracts mode emits inventory JSON, diagnostics, docs, and app-local UserAuth/AdminAuth contracts; missing Gemfile/gem/model/schema and ambiguous scopes fail closed; generated contracts compile; generator smoke covers discovery, write mode, review markers, manifest ownership, and failure cases.
close_reason: Implemented Devise-specific deterministic adoption: Bundler/route/model/schema inventory, app-local UserAuth contracts, docs/diagnostics, fail-closed smoke coverage, Haxe compile checks, and golden snapshots for generated artifacts.

title: Add Devise route integration and parity
description: Implement top-level, no-options Haxe-owned Devise route declarations using DeviseRoutes.deviseFor(UserAuth.scope). Generated Devise scope fields must carry compiler-readable route metadata; the route macro must require a direct generated static scope field, emit ordinary devise_for :resource, record semantic mapping expectations in the Haxe route manifest, and continue generating route helper externs exclusively from actual Rails route output. Do not reproduce Devise's helper matrix in Haxe/RailsHx; Devise expands routes and Rails remains the helper oracle.
acceptance_criteria: DeviseRoutes.deviseFor(UserAuth.scope) compiles for direct generated scope fields and emits normal devise_for :users; literals, locals, function calls, constructed DeviseScope values, options, nested calls, missing metadata, stale metadata, and non-authorable adopted mappings fail with precise diagnostics; .railshx/routes.haxe.json records a semantic deviseFor declaration; route generator snapshots cover actual Devise routes from Rails output; route parity/facts work is tracked or implemented without guessing Devise helper matrices; Rails-owned devise_for route sync remains unchanged.
close_reason: Implemented no-options DeviseRoutes.deviseFor(UserAuth.scope) MVP: generated scope metadata, macro validation, Rails route IR/emitter/manifest support, exact devise_for output, Rails-owned Devise route helper fixture/snapshot coverage, and docs. Follow-ups tracked: haxe.ruby-443 for typed only/skip split mappings, haxe.ruby-4df for Ripper-backed route-authorability metadata, and haxe.ruby-2ke for Devise mapping facts/parity/manifest hardening.

title: Fold DeviseHx design into docs and inventory schema
description: Fold the GPT 5.5 Pro DeviseHx design review into durable repo docs and define the initial deterministic inventory schema for Devise adoption. This locks the companion-layer contract before implementation starts.
acceptance_criteria: DeviseHx architecture docs are committed and linked from the docs index/gem-layer guide; inventory schema draft covers gem/version, scopes, models, modules, helpers, route resources, schema status, unknowns, and unsafe boundaries; escape-hatch policy and greenfield/existing flows are documented; parent bead notes point to the folded design.
close_reason: Closed

title: Design RailsHx Haxe-level Hotwire package
description: Design and implement a Haxe-level RailsHx Hotwire library that gives apps typed, ergonomic helpers for common Turbo/Hotwire behavior instead of ad-hoc app-local DOM wiring. Start from the todoapp composer needs: Enter submits, Shift+Enter newline, clear-on-success, typed Turbo lifecycle hooks, stream/source status, shared selectors, requestSubmit compatibility, and form/session affordances. The package should sit under std/rails/hotwire or a clearly justified std/rails/turbo/dom split, emit normal Rails/Turbo/browser behavior, avoid raw js.Syntax.code in app examples, and use Genes-friendly Haxe APIs.
acceptance_criteria: Design doc compares rails.turbo, rails.dom, and a possible rails.hotwire namespace; todoapp consumes at least one higher-level typed Hotwire helper instead of hand-rolled binding where practical; std inventory/docs updated; examples document the UX and type-safety; Playwright covers Enter submit, Shift+Enter newline, clear-on-success, and native Turbo stream status; no duplicate client-side rendering path is introduced.
close_reason: Added the initial rails.hotwire Haxe-level helper layer with TextAreaComposer, refactored the todoapp chat composer to consume it, documented the rails.turbo/rails.dom/rails.hotwire split, registered std inventory entries, and verified Rails/Playwright todoapp gates.

title: Dogfood ActionCable late-subscriber Turbo Stream catch-up
description: Fix the todoapp chatroom race where a browser tab that had rendered the room but had not yet completed ActionCable subscription could miss a broadcast with no visible state. Keep the solution Rails-native: ActionCable transports, Rails/HHX renders a Turbo Stream snapshot, Turbo mutates the DOM, and Haxe owns typed orchestration/hooks.
acceptance_criteria: Todoapp chat panel exposes visible live/sync/reconnect state; GET /chat_messages returns a Turbo Stream snapshot rendered from typed HHX; Haxe JS syncs that snapshot after Cable connect using typed @:await; Playwright covers two-browser broadcast and late-subscriber catch-up; Rails tests cover the snapshot endpoint.
close_reason: accepted: todoapp now exposes Live/Syncing/Reconnecting state, GET /chat_messages serves a typed HHX Turbo Stream snapshot, Haxe JS syncs after Cable connect with @:await, and Playwright covers both two-browser broadcast and late-subscriber catch-up. Verified with npm run test:todoapp-rails, rake todoapp:test, rake todoapp:playwright, npm run test:snapshots, npm run format:haxe:check, and git diff --check.

title: Design full-stack typed Hotwire contracts for RailsHx
description: Design a RailsHx full-stack Hotwire authoring layer that leverages Haxe on both backend and frontend: shared typed ActionCable payloads, Turbo Stream targets/actions, HHX partial locals, DOM hooks, routes, params, and Playwright selectors should be generated or inferred from common Haxe contracts. The goal is better-than-vanilla Rails UX while keeping output idiomatic Rails/Turbo/ActionCable.
acceptance_criteria: A design doc compares client-rendered Turbo streams, server-rendered Turbo::StreamsChannel broadcasts, typed HHX partial rendering, typed stream targets, and generated Haxe JS subscription helpers. The design identifies macros/generators that remove selector/payload/target repetition, fail compile-time on drift, keep Rails-native output, and defines implementation beads. Todoapp chatroom is used as the motivating dogfood example.
close_reason: accepted: added docs/railshx-full-stack-hotwire-design.md comparing server-rendered streams, client-rendered streams, typed HHX locals, typed stream targets, generated subscription helpers, diagnostics, phases, and todoapp chatroom motivation; linked from RailsHx docs/roadmaps.

title: Add optional ActionCable live chat dogfood to todoapp_rails
description: Extend the todoapp RailsHx chatroom beyond the current Turbo mutation seam into a true ActionCable live-update dogfood slice once the app should exercise channel/broadcast surfaces end to end.
acceptance_criteria: Todoapp chat messages broadcast to another browser session without manual refresh; Haxe owns typed channel/stream/payload contracts where supported; Rails receives normal ActionCable artifacts; browser tests cover two-page live update behavior; docs explain why ActionCable is optional beyond the existing Turbo Stream form mutation seam; snapshots and runtime tests cover generated channel/broadcast artifacts.
close_reason: Implemented typed ActionCable live chat dogfood in todoapp_rails: Haxe-owned channel/stream/payload contracts, Rails ActionCable materializer wiring, Turbo Stream client rendering, Rails channel/request tests, and Playwright two-browser realtime coverage.

title: Design reusable DeviseHx companion gem layer
description: Design and implement the reusable companion-package path for popular Rails gems, using Devise as the first production-grade example. The generic hxruby:adopt --gem lane can generate app-local reviewed contracts; this task defines how to extract hardened reusable layers such as DeviseHx with typed model modules, controller helpers, route helpers, current_user typing, install docs, and runtime seam tests.
acceptance_criteria: Before implementation, ask GPT 5.5 Pro for a focused design review using this repo, ../haxe.elixir.codex, ../haxe.compilerdev.reference/rails, ../haxe.compilerdev.reference/ruby, and Devise source/docs as references. The resulting plan must identify deterministic extraction inputs, typed Haxe API shapes, unsafe/dynamic boundaries, generator templates, tests, and bead mutations. DeviseHx must install cleanly into a RailsHx app, keep Devise runtime ownership in Ruby/Bundler, expose typed Haxe contracts for authentication helpers and model/controller integration, document LLM-assisted review workflow, and pass Haxe compile plus Rails runtime/parity gates.
notes: GPT 5.5 Pro DeviseHx design review has been folded into docs/railshx-devisehx-design.md with a deterministic inventory schema at docs/railshx-devisehx-inventory.schema.json. Implementation is decomposed into child beads haxe.ruby-bjv.6.4.1 through haxe.ruby-bjv.6.4.10 covering core package, extractor/generator, routes, controllers/current-user, model/schema validation, HHX, sanitizer/mailer/test seams, dogfood app, and package hardening. Current-user flow hardening now respects Rails skip_before_action semantics: strict currentRequired analysis honors skipBeforeAction(UserAuth.authenticate, ...) and has a focused DeviseHx controller smoke regression.
close_reason: Reusable DeviseHx companion layer is decomposed and implemented through the completed child beads: design review/docs, core typed package, deterministic extractor/adoption generator, routes, controller/current-user typing, model/schema validation, HHX helpers, sanitizer/mailer/test seams, dogfood app, and the incubated package/release lane.

comments.author: Marcelo Serpa
comments.text: Added the repo-level DeviseHx GPT 5.5 Pro design prompt and linked it from the gem-layer guide, docs index, README, and generated RailsHx starter docs. Implementation of reusable DeviseHx remains intentionally blocked until the GPT design review is supplied and folded into bead mutations.

title: Dogfood route parity core as Haxe-authored Ruby
description: Move the deterministic route parity comparison core behind hxruby:routes into Haxe-authored code once the Ruby implementation has stabilized. Keep the Rails/Rake/generator adapter Ruby-native, but compile the pure manifest/Rails-route comparison library through haxe.ruby so the route tooling dogfoods the compiler and showcases typed Haxe that emits idiomatic Ruby.
acceptance_criteria: A Haxe-authored route parity core consumes the Haxe route manifest and normalized Rails route output, compiles to Ruby, and is used by the Ruby-native hxruby:routes task adapter. Existing Ruby parity behavior and diagnostics are preserved or improved. Snapshot/smoke coverage proves the generated Ruby is idiomatic and the parity failure cases still catch missing route, wrong target, wrong path, wrong verb, wrong explicit alias, and opaque raw-route rejection. Docs explain why the Rails task adapter remains Ruby-native while deterministic tooling is Haxe-authored dogfood.
close_reason: Closed

title: Add RailsHx @:async/@:await client sugar on Genes output
description: Build the RailsHx Haxe-authored JavaScript async/await authoring layer on top of the Genes ES module client lane. Reuse/adapt PhoenixHx patterns from ../haxe.elixir.codex where they fit Rails importmap/Turbo semantics.
acceptance_criteria: Typed @:async/@:await or equivalent helper syntax compiles to native JS async/await through Genes; todoapp demonstrates a meaningful async Turbo/browser flow; generated JS readability/snapshot or smoke coverage proves async output; docs explain the authoring pattern and fallback boundaries.
close_reason: Implemented typed RailsHx async/await client authoring on the Genes ES module lane with reflaxe.js.Async helpers, todoapp async Turbo flash behavior, generated starter support, smoke/readability assertions, browser coverage, and docs.

title: Investigate RailsHx Turbo same-page redirect browser teardown stall
description: The todoapp Playwright suite stalls around browser form mutations that redirect back to /todos. After a POST completes quickly and Rails follows with a fast redirected GET, a subsequent browser wait or fresh GET can hang until Playwright tears down the page. This reproduced for Turbo Drive POST redirects and for explicit data-turbo=false full-page redirects. The current browser spec skips the mutating form journey with this bead ID; Rails runtime/controller tests cover create/session behavior in the meantime. Investigate the root cause and prefer a Rails/Turbo-idiomatic fix such as proper Turbo Stream responses, clearer typed Turbo form helpers, importmap/Turbo lifecycle fixes, or Playwright/server teardown hardening rather than relying on skips.
acceptance_criteria: Mutating todoapp Playwright form flows are unskipped and pass consistently; full todoapp Playwright suite passes regardless of test order; session/create forms use a documented Rails/Turbo-idiomatic pattern; POST/redirect/render requests complete promptly without waiting for browser teardown; Rails runtime/controller coverage remains green; docs/tests explain the chosen pattern.
close_reason: Fixed by using Turbo-enabled forms, typed Turbo Stream responses for todo/session mutations, and a robust Puma launch for Playwright keep-alive load.

title: Adopt Genes-style async await for RailsHx client JS
description: Investigate and port/adapt the PhoenixHx Genes client JavaScript setup from ../haxe.elixir.codex so RailsHx Haxe-authored JS can use typed @:async/@:await sugar and emit clean modern ES module async/await output for Turbo/importmap workflows.
acceptance_criteria: Audit ../haxe.elixir.codex Genes integration, including vendor/genes, std/reflaxe/js/Async.hx, and async snapshot tests; decide whether to vendor Genes, depend on it, or reuse a shared package; RailsHx build-client.hxml can opt into Genes-style output; @:async/@:await or equivalent typed sugar compiles to native async/await; todoapp client JS has at least one meaningful async/Turbo example; generated JS snapshot/browser coverage proves clean async output; docs explain the Rails importmap/Turbo workflow and fallback to vanilla JS if Genes is unavailable.
notes: Expanded design scope after PhoenixHx comparison: RailsHx client JS should be a Rails-native typed abstraction layer over Turbo/importmap, not just Haxe syntax for vanilla DOM code. PhoenixHx uses app-local build-client.hxml with -lib genes, --macro genes.Generator.use(), js-es=6/js-unflatten/source maps, typed hook registries shared with HXX/templates, and explicit docs that the server compiler stays separate from the JS target. RailsHx should mirror the architecture: generated Rails apps keep Rails/importmap/Turbo ownership, compile Haxe client code through app-local build-client.hxml, prefer Genes when available for readable ES modules and future @:async/@:await, keep plain Haxe JS as a minimal fallback, and provide typed Turbo abstractions that reduce selector/event/header/form boilerplate compared with JS/TS. Acceptance should include typed Turbo controller/behavior helpers, shared hook registries used by HHX + Haxe JS + Playwright, todoapp async/Turbo example, generated JS snapshot/readability gate, app-generator client-mode docs, and no hidden parallel Turbo runtime.\n\n2026-06-18: Landed the Genes ES-module foundation for RailsHx client JS: vendored Genes, added scoped haxe_libraries stubs, switched todoapp build-client.hxml to Genes, rewrote generated Genes relative imports to Rails importmap bare specifiers, pinned app/javascript/railshx modules, updated the RailsHx install generator/client rake flow, and covered todoapp browser/production plus haxelib/gem packaging. Remaining scope is the higher-level @:async/@:await authoring sugar and a richer async Turbo example, tracked by haxe.ruby-ae6.2.
close_reason: Completed the RailsHx Genes client JS adoption: vendored/package-covered Genes ES modules, importmap rewrite flow, typed Turbo todoapp integration, native @:async/await authoring via reflaxe.js.Async, browser/production/package gates, and docs.

title: Add RailsHx template and typed test generator entrypoints
description: Add focused Rails generator entrypoints for typed HHX templates and Haxe-authored Rails tests after the scaffold/model/controller contracts are stable. These generators should create Haxe source/contracts only; Rails continues to render/run the generated Rails-native artifacts.
acceptance_criteria: bin/rails generate hxruby:template PATH creates a typed HHX view/partial skeleton with checked locals and Template.of/Template.existing guidance; generator refuses unsafe paths and does not write raw ERB as source; bin/rails generate hxruby:test NAME creates Haxe-authored Rails test skeletons using the compiler-erased @:railsTests define() DSL and the appropriate ModelTestCase/RequestTestCase base; generated tests lower to ordinary Rails test files and run via bin/rails test; docs/help explain when to use vanilla Rails tests versus Haxe-authored tests; generator depends on haxe.ruby-skz for request/controller test helper maturity.
close_reason: Added focused RailsHx template and Haxe-authored Rails test generator entrypoints. hxruby:template generates typed HHX view/partial sources with checked locals and no raw ERB authoring; hxruby:test generates @:railsTests define() model/request test skeletons that lower to ordinary Rails/Minitest. Wired shared Ruby implementations, Rails generator adapters, scripts, Rake tasks, docs, starter AGENTS guidance, package checks, and smoke coverage.

title: Add migration-history collision and adoption reports
description: Add migration inventory and collision reporting so RailsHx can coexist with existing Rails migration history while preferring schema adoption over historical migration translation.
acceptance_criteria: hxruby:adopt --migrations --discover or hxruby:doctor reports db/migrate timestamps/classes, RailsHx-owned generated migration files, non-owned Ruby migrations, duplicate classes, duplicate timestamps, and pending collisions before compile; hxruby:migration --timestamp existing fails with actionable diagnostics; docs explain why schema adoption from current db/schema.rb is preferred over translating historical migrations by default; no historical migration conversion runs unless a future explicit converter bead is implemented; tests cover collision reports and non-owned migration preservation.
notes: Implemented report-only migration-history adoption: `hxruby:adopt --migrations --discover` inventories db/migrate, classifies Rails-owned versus RailsHx-owned files, reports duplicate timestamps/classes, and points users back to schema adoption for current model contracts. `hxruby:migration --timestamp` now refuses any existing timestamp under db/migrate even when the filename differs, and it refuses duplicate migration classes. Existing `hxruby:doctor` duplicate timestamp/class diagnostics remain in place. Docs and smokes cover non-owned migration preservation and the no-historical-conversion policy.
close_reason: Completed migration-history reporting and collision hardening without adding historical migration conversion. Adoption can inventory and classify migrations, doctor reports duplicates, and new Haxe migration snapshots fail closed on existing timestamps/classes.

title: Design and prototype Haxe-authored Playwright/Vitest RailsHx tests
description: Prototype optional Haxe-authored browser/client tests that reuse RailsHx typed DOM hooks, Turbo contracts, route constants, and client payloads while lowering to Playwright/Vitest-compatible artifacts.
acceptance_criteria: Design decision is made for TypeScript emission vs Haxe-to-JS plus typings; todoapp has one Haxe-authored browser test example or a documented deferral with rationale; generated artifacts are ordinary Playwright/Vitest-compatible files; vanilla Playwright tests remain first-class.
close_reason: Implemented first Haxe-authored Playwright slice. Added typed rails.test.playwright facades, todoapp e2e_haxe spec compiled through Genes ES modules, Playwright-discoverable generated wrapper, haxe-playwright smoke, full todoapp Playwright integration, docs, and std inventory entries. Vanilla TypeScript Playwright specs remain first-class.

title: Design optional Haxe-authored Ruby and JS test layers
description: Design RailsHx optional typed Haxe-authored testing layers for Ruby/Rails runtime tests and JS/browser tests, inspired by haxe.elixir.codex testing ergonomics but adapted to Rails/Ruby and modern JS tooling. Vanilla Ruby/Rails tests must remain first-class because generated code should be idiomatic Ruby.
acceptance_criteria: A design doc compares vanilla Rails tests, Haxe-authored Ruby/Rails tests, and Haxe-authored JS/browser tests; APIs show how typed model fields, route helpers, params/template refs, DOM hooks, Turbo contracts, and client payloads are reused; examples include todoapp tests in both vanilla and typed Haxe-authored forms; output lowers to ordinary Minitest/RSpec/Rails and Playwright/Vitest-compatible artifacts; implementation beads are filed for the chosen first slice.
close_reason: Completed design for optional Haxe-authored Ruby/Rails and JS/browser test layers. Added docs/railshx-haxe-authored-testing-design.md comparing vanilla Rails tests, Haxe-authored Rails tests, vanilla JS/browser tests, and Haxe-authored browser tests; included Rails/Minitest, request-test, and Playwright-shaped examples; clarified output ownership and todoapp migration path; filed implementation beads haxe.ruby-0wk, haxe.ruby-skz, and haxe.ruby-a3x.

title: Harden ActiveJob adapter-specific discard and failure diagnostics
description: Follow-up from haxe.ruby-71o. Investigate and harden adapter-specific ActiveJob behavior beyond Rails' test adapter, especially discard_on runtime assertions, failed/discarded job diagnostics, queue adapter differences for Solid Queue/Sidekiq/GoodJob/custom adapters, and documentation for what RailsHx guarantees versus app-owned integration tests.
acceptance_criteria: At least one discard_on runtime assertion is covered or explicitly deferred with Rails-version rationale; adapter-specific assumptions are documented; any new typed queue/adapter API is justified by app-facing UX; generated Rails runtime tests or app-level fixtures cover the supported path; docs link from the ActiveJob guide.
notes: Paused before implementation; user redirected to RailsHx compile-then-delegate task workflow. Leave open for a future ActiveJob pass.
close_reason: Added typed ActiveJob discard runtime coverage: DeserializationError.raise(...) lowers to native Rails raise, DiscardProbeJob exercises discard_on, Rails ActiveJob::TestHelper asserts discarded work, and docs/audit clarify that third-party adapter diagnostics remain app-owned unless a future typed adapter API is justified.

title: Design typed selected aggregate result objects for RailsHx
description: Follow-up to haxe.ruby-6ri. Design and implement selected aggregate aliases/result row shapes on top of RailsHx projection/expression layers, e.g. count/sum/avg/min/max aliases that infer typed row fields without raw select strings. Cover alias naming, filesystem/docs examples, generated Ruby, and negative tests for wrong owner/type/raw SQL.
acceptance_criteria: Typed aggregate projection API infers result object shapes; common count/sum/avg/min/max alias examples compile without raw SQL; wrong-owner/wrong-type/raw-string cases fail during Haxe compilation; docs explain generated Ruby and when to use explicit Sql.* escapes instead.
close_reason: Implemented Projection.group for typed selected grouped aggregate result rows. Specs infer Array<{...}> row shapes from grouped field refs and Aggregate count/sum/average/minimum/maximum expressions, lower to Rails group(...).pluck(...), reject wrong-owner/raw-string/non-grouped-field cases, and update docs/tests.

title: Audit RailsHx DSL ergonomics under contextual valid-Haxe design rule
description: Review existing RailsHx/RubyHx DSLs and macro surfaces against the new contextual valid-Haxe design rule: use legal Haxe syntax, semantic anchor names, typed refs over strings, minimal ceremony, and compiler-erased marker calls where appropriate. Prioritize controller lifecycle, ActiveRecord scopes/query helpers, model metadata, migrations, HHX/components, mailer/job/channel macros, generators, and Ruby extension interop. Propose/implement migrations where a DSL is metadata-heavy, stringly, vaguely named, or less ergonomic than the equivalent Ruby/Rails API without a type-safety reason.
acceptance_criteria: Inventory existing public DSLs with current shape, desired shape, and migration risk; identify which should remain metadata for compatibility; update docs/examples for canonical shapes; add compile tests for at least one improved DSL beyond controller lifecycle if implementation is in scope; compatibility aliases or deprecation notes are documented.
close_reason: Completed RailsHx DSL ergonomics audit with docs inventory and implemented ActiveJob contextual lifecycle DSL. New canonical job API uses valid-Haxe static lifecycle blocks with typed exception refs, compiler-erased marker calls, legacy metadata compatibility, updated examples/docs, negative compile coverage, inventory/package coverage, and focused gates passing.

title: Design typed aggregate aliases and having for RailsHx
description: Design and implement typed aggregate/projection expressions on top of the RailsHx Expr layer. Cover count/sum/avg/min/max aliases, selected result typing, group/having predicates, result object shape, and generated Ruby. Avoid raw select/group/having strings by default; if an escape is needed it must route through the explicit Sql.* policy.
close_reason: Implemented typed Aggregate expression builders and Group.countHaving aggregate having predicates; docs/tests cover generated Ruby and wrong-owner/raw-string failures. Follow-up haxe.ruby-d4u tracks selected aggregate result object aliases.

title: Unify RailsHx comparison predicates on Expr backend
description: Refactor existing whereGt/whereGte/whereLt/whereLte and negated variants to share the Expr/Predicate lowering path internally while keeping the Rails-shaped convenience API. Preserve existing generated Ruby and diagnostics where possible. Add regression tests so future predicate builders compose consistently.
notes: Working this as the immediate follow-up to Expr/Predicate. Scope: keep public whereGt/whereGte/whereLt/whereLte and negated variants unchanged, but route compiler lowering through the same expression predicate helper used by whereExpr(...). Add/keep smoke coverage proving generated Ruby is unchanged and update docs/notes so future predicates extend one path.
close_reason: Unified existing whereGt/whereGte/whereLt/whereLte and negated convenience predicates with the Expr/Predicate compiler backend by routing both authoring surfaces through activeRecordExpressionPredicateArg. Generated Ruby shape remains unchanged; ActiveRecord smoke verifies existing comparison output and Expr predicate output. Docs now state the shared backend and recommended usage split.

title: Harden gradual adoption and mixed Rails interop for production
description: Make RailsHx safe for incrementally adopting existing Ruby/ERB/Rails apps and for Ruby code consuming Haxe output.
acceptance_criteria: Examples cover Ruby consuming Haxe services/templates, Haxe consuming existing Ruby/ERB/components, RBS/source-backed contract generation, missing-file fail-closed behavior, and runtime checks for interop boundaries; docs explain recommended migration paths.
close_reason: All gradual-adoption child work is complete: schema adoption, migration-history reports, generator-assisted gem-layer adoption, and reusable DeviseHx companion layer/release lane. Existing docs and smoke/runtime gates cover mixed Ruby/ERB/Haxe interop, generated contracts, fail-closed behavior, and migration paths.

title: Publish RailsHx production readiness checklist and support contract
description: Document what production-ready means, current status, required gates, and how users should evaluate risk before adopting RailsHx.
acceptance_criteria: Docs state current maturity, production readiness criteria, mandatory commands, known blockers, supported Ruby/Rails/Haxe/Node versions, release/support expectations, and links to all production-readiness beads.
close_reason: Published the RailsHx production readiness checklist/support contract in docs/railshx-production-readiness.md, linked it from README/roadmap/compatibility, and added AGENTS guidance requiring production blockers to be tracked under haxe.ruby-bjv.

title: Implement RailsHx typed ActiveRecord readOnly
description: Add typed ActiveRecord readOnly support on models and relations, preserving Haxe casing while emitting Rails-native readonly().
acceptance_criteria: Todo.readOnly() and relation.readOnly() type-check as Relation<Todo, criteria>, emit readonly() calls, preserve chaining after readOnly, and active_record_model smoke/docs/snapshots/package checks are updated.
close_reason: Implemented typed ActiveRecord readOnly on generated model stubs and Relation, preserving Haxe camelCase while emitting Rails-native readonly(), with ActiveRecord smoke, docs, inventory/roadmap/compatibility, and snapshot updates.

title: Implement RailsHx typed ActiveRecord reverseOrder
description: Add typed ActiveRecord reverseOrder support on models and relations, preserving Haxe casing while emitting Rails-native reverse_order().
acceptance_criteria: Todo.reverseOrder() and relation.reverseOrder() type-check as Relation<Todo, criteria>, emit reverse_order() calls, preserve chaining after reverseOrder, and active_record_model smoke/docs/snapshots/package checks are updated.
close_reason: Implemented typed ActiveRecord reverseOrder on generated model stubs and Relation, preserving Haxe camelCase while emitting Rails-native reverse_order(), with ActiveRecord smoke, docs, inventory/roadmap/compatibility, and snapshot updates.

title: Implement RailsHx typed ActiveRecord none relation
description: Add typed ActiveRecord none support on models and relations so RailsHx can express null relations while preserving relation chaining.
acceptance_criteria: Todo.none() and relation.none() type-check as Relation<Todo, criteria>, emit Rails-native none() calls, preserve chaining after none, and active_record_model smoke/docs/snapshots/package checks are updated.
close_reason: Implemented typed ActiveRecord none on generated model stubs and Relation, preserving typed chaining while emitting Rails-native none() calls, with ActiveRecord smoke, docs, inventory/roadmap/compatibility, and snapshot updates.

title: Implement RailsHx typed ActiveRecord relation merge
description: Add typed ActiveRecord Relation.merge support so same-model relations compose with Rails-native merge(...) output and wrong-model relations fail during Haxe compilation.
acceptance_criteria: Todo.where(...).merge(Todo.where(...)) type-checks and emits normal Rails .merge(...); relation chains remain typed after merge; User relation operands are rejected by Haxe; active_record_model smoke/docs/snapshots/package checks are updated.
close_reason: Implemented typed ActiveRecord Relation.merge, kept generated Ruby as Rails-native .merge(...), added same-model relation composition coverage, mixed-model negative compile coverage, docs, compatibility/roadmap/inventory updates, and snapshots.

title: Implement RailsHx typed ActiveRecord relation or
description: Add typed ActiveRecord Relation.or support so same-model relations compose with Rails-native or(...) output and wrong-model relations fail during Haxe compilation.
acceptance_criteria: Todo.where(...).or(Todo.where(...)) type-checks and emits normal Rails .or(...); relation chains remain typed after or; User relation operands are rejected by Haxe; active_record_model smoke/docs/snapshots/package checks are updated.
close_reason: Implemented typed ActiveRecord Relation.or with @:native("or") output, added same-model relation composition coverage, mixed-model negative compile coverage, docs, compatibility/roadmap/inventory updates, and snapshots.

title: Implement RailsHx typed multi-field order
description: Add typed multi-field ActiveRecord order/reorder support via generated Order refs, preserving owner checks and Rails-shaped output.
acceptance_criteria: Order.many([Todo.f.title.asc(), Todo.f.id.desc()]) compiles as Order<Todo>; Todo.order(...) and relation.reorder(...) lower to Rails order(title: :asc, id: :desc)/reorder(...); wrong-owner mixed orders fail during Haxe compilation; active_record_model smoke, query docs, README, roadmap/compatibility, snapshots, and package/inventory checks are updated.
close_reason: Implemented typed multi-field ActiveRecord ordering via Order.many, added model-level order stubs, lowered arrays of typed order refs to Rails-native order/reorder keyword args, covered mixed-model failures, updated ActiveRecord smoke, docs, compatibility/roadmap/inventory, and snapshots.

title: Implement RailsHx typed numeric ActiveRecord aggregations
description: Add typed numeric aggregation helpers for RailsHx ActiveRecord queries. Start with Rails-shaped sum/average over generated model field refs, preserve model/relation source typing, reject wrong-owner fields, and document generated Rails Ruby.
acceptance_criteria: Todo.sum(Todo.f.userId) and relation.sum(Todo.f.userId) compile and lower to sum(:user_id); average over supported numeric fields compiles with a nullable numeric return; wrong-owner aggregation fields fail during Haxe compilation; active_record_model smoke, query docs, example README, roadmap/compatibility, snapshots, inventory/package checks are updated.
close_reason: Implemented v1 typed ActiveRecord sum/average for Int field refs on models and relations, lowered them to Rails sum(:field)/average(:field), added positive and negative ActiveRecord smoke coverage, docs, inventory, roadmap/compatibility, and snapshots.

title: Implement RailsHx named multi-field Projection.pluck
description: Implement the chosen named-object multi-field projection API from docs/railshx-projections-grouping-design.md. Projection.pluck(source, {key: Model.f.field, ...}) should return Array<{...}> with field-value types and lower through ActiveRecord pluck(:field, ...).

title: Refactor RubyCompiler into focused RailsHx compiler modules
description:
RubyCompiler.hx now owns core Ruby lowering plus RailsHx-specific surfaces such as ActionView/HHX, Turbo/Turbo Streams, routes, migrations, tests, generators/artifacts, and Rails controller/model lowering. Plan and execute an incremental extraction into focused modules so the compiler stays maintainable and easier to reason about. This refactor must be done very carefully with comprehensive regression coverage before and during extraction.

Direction:
- Keep RubyCompiler as the orchestration/root Reflaxe compiler entrypoint.
- Extract target-neutral Ruby helpers separately from RailsHx-specific lowering.
- Move Rails template/HHX lowering into an ActionView/RailsTemplates compiler module.
- Move Turbo/Turbo Streams/Hotwire lowering into a RailsTurbo compiler module.
- Move routes, migrations, Rails tests, and artifact ownership into focused Rails compiler modules where appropriate.
- Preserve current generated output and diagnostics during extraction; this is structure-first, behavior-preserving refactoring unless a subtask explicitly changes behavior.
- Prefer many small extraction commits over a large rewrite. Each commit should move one coherent lowering surface and keep tests green.

Regression-safety requirements:
- Add or audit golden snapshot coverage before extracting each surface. If exact generated output is the contract, snapshots must prove it.
- Keep focused smoke/runtime tests for seams snapshots cannot prove: Rails boot/autoload, template lookup, routes, migrations, browser/Turbo behavior, and diagnostics.
- Add negative compile tests for diagnostics when moving code that emits Context.error/Context.warning.
- Use characterization tests before refactoring ambiguous code paths; do not rely on manual inspection of generated Ruby.
- No generated-output drift is allowed unless a subtask explicitly documents the intentional behavior change and updates snapshots/docs.
- After each extraction slice, run the relevant focused gates plus full snapshot suite when output emitters moved.

Acceptance criteria:
- A short design doc maps current RubyCompiler responsibilities to proposed modules and extraction order, including test coverage required before moving each surface.
- Baseline snapshot/smoke coverage for the first extracted surface exists before the extraction commit.
- At least one low-risk RailsHx surface is extracted behind a small typed API with no generated-output changes.
- Snapshot/smoke tests prove generated Ruby/ERB/JS artifacts are unchanged for extracted surfaces.
- Diagnostics tests prove moved error paths still point at useful source positions/messages.
- Docs/AGENTS guidance explains that new RailsHx compiler surfaces should prefer focused modules instead of adding more bulk directly to RubyCompiler.hx.
notes: 2026-06-29: While replacing the DeviseHx-specific no-emit marker with generic @:rubyNoEmit, confirmed RubyCompiler still contains embedded DeviseHx lowering/validation for auth helpers, route metadata, parameter sanitizer helpers, HHX auth links/errors, and currentRequired flow analysis. Extraction plan should include a companion-layer lowering/registry boundary so DeviseHx and future gem layers can contribute typed metadata handlers without package-name logic in the core compiler. Until that exists, new companion-specific behavior should use generic metadata contracts and avoid adding more package checks to RubyCompiler.
close_reason: Closed

title: Fix Ruby lambda statement-body local lowering
description: While implementing Reflect.makeVarArgs coverage, a Haxe lambda with a block-local accumulator compiled to a Ruby lambda body that referenced the final local without emitting its declaration or loop statements. Preserve block-local declarations/control flow inside Ruby lambda bodies, then add focused coverage so statement-bodied lambdas used as values do not collapse to the final expression.
close_reason: Closed

notes:
Enabled the adapted upstream Std.unit.hx parity fixture in the Ruby unitstd lane. Added support fixtures for Haxe toString, enum, class inheritance, and interface checks; added macro support for upstream unspec markers and in-expression assertions. Ruby output now emits real Ruby superclass/module relationships for Haxe-owned classes and interfaces so Std.isOfType works without a broad wrapper runtime. Std.string now uses HXRuby.stringify for semantic cases including null, enums, and Haxe toString while keeping HXRuby compact and purpose-driven. Refreshed snapshots and todoapp smoke expectations. Local gates passed: format:haxe:check, test:unitstd-ruby, test:runtime-minitest, test:stdlib-mvp, test:runtime-core, test:stdlib-inventory, test:gap-report, test:snapshots, test:m1, test:m2, public:precommit, test:todoapp-rails.

The compiler now relies on precise require_relative runtime requires for native artifacts, smoke tests reject the legacy initializer, and the todoapp snapshot manifest was refreshed. Additional local gates passed: test:active-job, test:rails-integration, test:rails-runtime, test:snapshots, public:precommit.
Follow-up from the amended CI run: production dogfood also needs the generated todoapp Rails application shell to ignore app/lib/railshx/runtime with Zeitwerk when app/lib is autoloaded/eager-loaded. The materializer now adds a normal Rails initializer in config/application.rb for that ignore rather than restoring the legacy hxruby_autoload initializer. Local gate passed: test:todoapp-production.
Follow-up from the full Ruby/Haxe matrix: updated remaining Ruby interop/extension smoke expectations that still assumed Std.string lowered to .to_s(), and regenerated the route parity Ruby artifact so it uses HXRuby.stringify for Haxe string semantics. Local gates passed: test:ruby-call-shapes, test:ruby-interop, test:ruby-extensions, test:routes-generator, and the post-route npm-test tail through test:gem-package.
close_reason: Std.unit.hx parity is enabled in the Ruby unitstd lane with semantic stringify/isOfType support and passing local gates.

title: Design optional RailsHx test-framework adapter DSL

Canonical syntax must remain valid Haxe and compiler-erased, lower to idiomatic target tests, preserve typed RailsHx assertions/fixtures/routes/models where possible, and keep framework-specific output behind explicit adapter metadata/generator configuration. Include design docs comparing Minitest/RSpec adapters, snapshot tests for generated test artifacts, negative compile tests for invalid DSL usage, and generator/docs flow for detecting the app's chosen test framework without forcing a dependency.

title: Add upstream Haxe unitstd runtime parity harness for Ruby target
description: Design and implement a low-priority Haxe stdlib portability validation layer for haxe.ruby by reusing a curated subset of upstream Haxe tests/unit/src/unitstd specs, compiling them through haxe.ruby, and executing the generated Ruby/Minitest or plain Ruby runtime lane. This is separate from RailsHx feature work and should validate that portable Haxe stdlib/domain code behaves as Haxe expects when emitted to Ruby.
acceptance_criteria: Audit ../haxe.elixir.codex test/upstream_unitstd, test/haxe_exunit/stdlib_parity, scripts/test-haxe-exunit-stdlib.sh, docs/08-roadmap/stdlib-parity/*, and scripts/sync-upstream-unitstd-specs.sh as the precedent; create a checked-in curated upstream unitstd subset with provenance/licensing notes and deterministic sync script; add a focused command such as npm run test:haxe-ruby-stdlib or test:unitstd-ruby that compiles Haxe-authored std parity tests to Ruby and executes them; document snapshot vs runtime responsibilities, with snapshots owning generated Ruby shape and unitstd runtime tests owning Haxe std semantics; include initial specs for high-leverage modules already implemented in haxe.ruby such as Array, StringTools/String, Math, Date, EReg, Lambda, Map/StringMap/IntMap/ObjectMap, haxe.io.Bytes, and Std; update docs/stdlib-ownership.md, docs/gap-report-guidance.md, and the production-readiness evidence once the lane exists; keep this P4 until RailsHx production gaps are further reduced.
notes: 2026-06-30: Captured the compact-HXRuby stdlib lesson in AGENTS.md and tightened existing stdlib lowering. Haxe Math.exp no longer emits a Ruby Math wrapper, preserving Ruby's native Math.exp instead of routing through HXRuby; Math.isNaN lowers directly in the generated shell; Std shell methods now use correct semantic helpers only where needed and direct Ruby for int/random. Removed the unused :exp entry from HXRuby's math helper table and refreshed snapshots. Local gates passed: format:haxe:check, test:runtime-minitest, test:runtime-core, test:stdlib-mvp, test:unitstd-ruby, test:m1, test:m2, test:stdlib-inventory, test:gap-report, test:snapshots, public:precommit, ci:release-contracts.\n\n2026-06-30: Tightened Std.parseInt/Std.parseFloat after reviewing the Math/HXRuby lesson. The compiler no longer emits Ruby-first Integer/Float rescue shortcuts because Ruby parses whole strings while Haxe Std parsing returns valid numeric prefixes. HXRuby.parse_int/parse_float are now compact semantic shims: regex the Haxe-valid prefix, then delegate conversion to Ruby. Added focused unitstd Ruby checks plus direct runtime assertions, documented the shell-vs-compiler-special-lowering rule in AGENTS.md, refreshed snapshots, and verified with test:runtime-minitest, test:stdlib-mvp, test:unitstd-ruby, test:runtime-core, test:m1, test:m2, test:todoapp-rails, test:stdlib-inventory, test:gap-report, ci:release-contracts, and public:precommit.
close_reason: Established the Ruby upstream unitstd runtime parity lane with checked-in provenance-tracked fixtures, deterministic sync script, npm run test:unitstd-ruby, docs for snapshot-vs-runtime responsibilities, production-readiness evidence, and passing focused gates. Baseline fixtures cover IntIterator, Math, String, StringBuf, StringTools, adapted Std, and haxe.io.BytesBuffer; staged high-leverage Array/Date/EReg/Lambda/Map fixture expansion is tracked separately as haxe.ruby-bjv.20.

title: R&D: investigate RailsHx to Phoenix/Elixir migration adapter path
description: Research whether RailsHx/Haxe can support an optional compile-time adapter/translation layer that lets parts of a RailsHx app, or eventually a whole RailsHx app, target Phoenix/Elixir. The goal is not immediate implementation: investigate whether Haxe can preserve idiomatic Rails authoring while enabling gradual migration to Phoenix/Elixir, mixed Ruby/Elixir components, or parallel idiomatic Haxe.Elixir authoring through adapters/translations. This should compare compile-level translation, generated boundary adapters, runtime interop, framework semantic gaps, data/schema/query portability, templates/components, controllers/routes, jobs/mailers/realtime, and gradual migration workflows.
acceptance_criteria: Before implementation starts, the user must run a GPT 5.5 Pro research pass and return insights plus proposed bead/plan mutations. The R&D output should document feasibility, recommended architecture options, risks, non-goals, required collaboration with haxe.elixir.codex/PhoenixHx, and concrete follow-up beads if the idea is worth pursuing.
notes: Blocked by the task's own acceptance criteria: implementation/design work must wait until the user runs the requested GPT 5.5 Pro research pass and returns feasibility notes plus proposed bead/plan mutations. Prompt was provided in-session on 2026-07-01.

title: Audit std helpers that can lower directly to idiomatic Ruby
description: Several std/Math and std/runtime helpers may delegate to HXRuby even when Ruby already has an equivalent receiver/module method, e.g. Math.abs -> value.abs. Audit similar cases and inline/direct-lower them when Ruby semantics match Haxe. Keep HXRuby helpers only where they encode Haxe-specific behavior, coercion/NaN/-0.0 edge cases, cross-version Ruby compatibility, or runtime reuse that materially improves correctness.
close_reason: Audited std Math helpers and direct-lowered Math.abs to value.abs and Math.isFinite to value.finite? because Ruby receiver semantics match Haxe for typed Float values. Kept HXRuby helpers where they still encode Haxe behavior, including min/max NaN propagation, domain-error-to-NaN math, pow, round, and isNaN for Integer-safe checks. Removed unused math_abs/math_finite? runtime helpers and added stdlib smoke assertions for the direct Ruby shapes. Verified with npm run test:runtime-minitest, npm run test:stdlib-mvp, UPDATE_SNAPSHOTS=1 npm run test:snapshots, npm test.
